elements.label: text

elements.text: of Section 9206.2(c)

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: 9206.4: Settling, reporting and data submission for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: loan modification (04/08/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Preparing to settle the loan modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan modification documents and settlement data submissions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Modification reporting and drafting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic solicitation and modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Preparing to settle the loan modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer should allow sufficient processing time to prepare the modification agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: and provide it to the Borrower for execution, so that the Borrower has sufficient time to
elements.label: text

elements.text: return it to the Servicer and make the first modified payment by its due date. The first
elements.label: text

elements.text: modified payment due date is the first day of the month following the final Trial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: month, or, if applicable, the first day of the month following a processing month.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Preparing the modification agreement and providing it to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Prepare an original modification agreement for the Borrower’s signature (see Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 76, Loan Modification for Delinquent Mortgages, for an example of a modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement and Exhibit 76A, Authorized Changes to Exhibit 76, Loan Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Delinquent Mortgages, for authorized changes to Exhibit 76)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The modification agreement:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must be revised as necessary to conform with federal, State and local law and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms of the modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must not include any language that requires the Borrower to waive rights he or
elements.label: text

elements.text: she may have under applicable law, including the Consumer Credit Protection
elements.label: text

elements.text: Act, as a condition of the modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must have a Modification Effective Date and a due date of the first payment due
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the Trial Period (the “First Modified Payment”) of the first day of the month
elements.label: text

elements.text: following the end of the Trial Period, or, if applicable, the first day of the month
elements.label: text

elements.text: following the end of the processing month
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must be in recordable form in the following circumstances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If State or local law requires the modification agreement be recorded to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforceable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Mortgage is secured by property located in New York
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the modification agreement must be recorded pursuant to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Servicer’s practice for modifying Mortgages in its portfolio is to create
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification agreements in recordable form
elements.label: text

elements.text: The new interest rate and new principal balance on the modified Mortgage are
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective retroactive to the first day of the month just prior to the First Modified
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment due date (i.e., to allow for payment of interest in arrears). (See below for
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on the “processing month option”.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Deliver to the Borrower an unsigned copy of the modification agreement, together
elements.label: text

elements.text: with any applicable riders and disclosures, and provide a date by which the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: must sign and return the executed modification agreements (and applicable riders and
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclosures)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Once the Borrower has signed the modification agreement and made the last required
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payment due during the Trial Period and the Servicer determines the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower otherwise remains in compliance with the terms of the Trial Period Plan,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must sign the modification agreement and return a copy with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: signatures to the Borrower in order for the modification to take effect. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may not sign the modification agreement until the Borrower has signed the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification agreement and complied with all requirements of the Trial Period Plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: While the Servicer is required to provide a date by which the modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement must be signed and returned, provided the Servicer receives the executed
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement by the Modification Effective Date, the modification will take effect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Prepare an assignment of rents rider, if applicable (see Section 9206.2(c))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Prepare a due-on-transfer rider, if applicable (see Section 9206.2(c))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Prepare a Modification Bankruptcy Disclosure Rider, if applicable (see Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Prepare any documents necessary to modify the Mortgage, including applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclosure statements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Arrange to obtain a title endorsement or similar title insurance product, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 9206.2(c) for specific information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Processing month option
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event the Borrower does not pay the final Trial Period payment on or before the
elements.label: text

elements.text: due date set forth in the Trial Period Plan Notice, then the Servicer may, at its option,
elements.label: text

elements.text: prepare the modification agreement such that the Modification Effective Date and the due
elements.label: text

elements.text: date of the First Modified Payment is the first day of the second month following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: final Trial Period month. However, in this case, interest will not begin to accrue at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modified interest rate on the modified principal balance until the first day of the month
elements.label: text

elements.text: following the final Trial Period month to accommodate the First Modified Payment’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment of interest in arrears (“processing month option”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If the final Trial Period payment is due March 1 and the Servicer elects the
elements.label: text

elements.text: option described above, the Borrower is not required to make any payment during April,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the First Modified Payment under the modification agreement is due on May 1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: During the month of March, interest will accrue at the current pre-modification rate under
elements.label: text

elements.text: the current loan documents, which may impact the amount of the modified principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: balance. The modified interest rate and the modified principal balance will take effect on
elements.label: text

elements.text: April 1. The First Modified Payment due on May 1 will include interest in arrears that
elements.label: text

elements.text: accrued during April on the modified principal balance at the modified interest rate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer elects this option, the Borrower will not be required to make an additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trial Period payment during the month (the “processing month”) between the final Trial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Period month and the month in which the First Modified Payment is due.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must treat all Borrowers the same in applying this option by selecting, in its
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion and evidenced by a written policy, the date by which the final Trial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment must be submitted (“cutoff date”) before the Servicer applies this option. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: cutoff date must be after the due date for the final Trial Period payment as set forth in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trial Period Plan Notice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event the Borrower does not submit funds during the processing month, the effects
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the processing month and attendant capitalization of arrearages on the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification agreement may not alter the Servicer’s previous determination of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s eligibility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer, when sending the modification agreement for signature, must include a
elements.label: text

elements.text: cover letter that informs the Borrower of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The delay of the Modification Effective Date and First Modified Payment due date by
elements.label: text

elements.text: one month, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The effects of the processing month, including, but not limited to, the delay in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective date of the modified interest rate, any increase in the delinquent interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: capitalized
elements.label: text

elements.text: EDR reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer elects to use the processing month option in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements under this Section 9206.4(a)(ii), the Servicer may report the interim month
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac through EDR. In doing so, the Servicer may report default action codes
elements.label: text

elements.text: “BF” under the Freddie Mac Flex Modification® Trial Period Plan. In addition, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must also report the Trial Period Plan Effective Date as the default action date
elements.label: text

elements.text: until the default action codes would no longer apply once the Mortgage is modified.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 9206.2(d) for information on Resolve® reporting of default action code
elements.label: text

elements.text: BF.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9102.6 for information on EDR and Exhibit 82, Electronic Default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting Transmission Code List, for descriptions of the default action codes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: default reason codes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages with temporary subsidy buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: When preparing and sending the modification agreement to the Borrower in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Section 9206.4(a) on a Mortgage with a temporary subsidy buydown plan, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must make the appropriate changes as necessary to reflect application of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown funds as a condition of completing the Mortgage modification, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Loan modification documents and settlement data submissions
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the Servicer has sent a copy of the fully executed modification agreement to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower as required by Section 9206.4(a)(i), the Servicer must comply with the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the modification agreement must be recorded (see Sections 9206.2(c) and 9206.4(a)),
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Submit the fully executed original modification agreement for recordation within five
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Days of receiving either the Borrower executed modification agreement or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the final Trial Period payment, whichever is later. (See Section 1401.3(d) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements pertaining to Electronic recording of paper post-closing documents.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the original Security Instrument was registered with MERS®, execute the
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Drafting

elements.text: (iv)Postsettlement discrepancies

elements.label: text

elements.text: settled with data elements matching these documents.

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Electronic solicitation and modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of this section:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms pertaining to electronic solicitation and modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: B
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower As applicable to eModification Agreements, in addition to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: definition contained in the Glossary, a person defined as a consumer
elements.label: text

elements.text: under E-SIGN, to the extent E-SIGN applies to a transaction. For the
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes of this section, “Borrower” also includes any and all persons
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligated under the terms of any applicable Note as the context shall
elements.label: text

elements.text: permit or require.
elements.label: text

elements.text: E
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Relating to technology having electrical, digital, magnetic, wireless,
elements.label: text

elements.text: optical, electromagnetic, or similar capabilities, as defined in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “UETA” and/or “E-SIGN”.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Signature
elements.label: text

elements.text: An “Electronic” sound, symbol or process attached to, or logically
elements.label: text

elements.text: associated with, a contract or other “Record” and executed or adopted
elements.label: text

elements.text: by a person with the intent to sign the “Record,” as defined in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “UETA” and/or “E-SIGN”.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: An action or set of actions occurring between two or more persons
elements.label: text

elements.text: relating to the conduct of business, commercial, or governmental
elements.label: text

elements.text: affairs, using “Electronic” means, as defined in the “UETA” and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: “E-SIGN”.
elements.label: text

elements.text: eModification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: an agreement that is an Electronic Record that complies with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable modification requirements of the Guide and includes, with
elements.label: text

elements.text: respect to paper Notes, Electronic modification agreements and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic assumption and release of liability agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: under Chapters 9206 and 8406, respectively.
elements.label: text

elements.text: in paper, and that the Borrower has agreed to issue it as a Transferable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Record.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Act (15 U.S. Code, Chapter 96).

elements.label: text

elements.text: an electronic or other medium and is retrievable in perceivable form

elements.text: as defined in the “UETA” and/or “E-SIGN.” A Record may be a

elements.text: paper or an “Electronic” document.

elements.text: (iii)Electronic solicitation

elements.text: Servicers may solicit Borrowers for a modification electronically and may add new

elements.text: (iv)eModification Agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The requirements of this Section 9206.4(d) apply only to eModification Agreements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: paper Notes and related Security Instruments. See Section 1402.5(c)(i) for requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: for eModification Agreements that modify an eMortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer allowing Borrowers to receive, transmit or electronically sign an
elements.label: text

elements.text: eModification Agreement represents and warrants to Freddie Mac that it has
elements.label: text

elements.text: complied with the requirements of the Guide and that the eModification Agreement is
elements.label: text

elements.text: authentic, its terms are valid and enforceable against the Borrower and the modified
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage complies with the requirements of Section 9206.2(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Restrictions on the use of an eModification Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: An eModification Agreement may be a Category 3 SMARTDoc (v1.02), unless this
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9206 requires the eModification Agreement to be recorded or in recordable
elements.label: text

elements.text: format and the recording jurisdiction does not support the Category 3 SMARTDoc
elements.label: text

elements.text: format.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may not use an eModification Agreement if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage, as modified by the eModification Agreement, would be invalid or
elements.label: text

elements.text: unenforceable or would no longer be in First Lien position
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The jurisdiction in which the eModification Agreement would be recorded, when
elements.label: text

elements.text: recordation is required, does not permit or provide for recordation of Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer is unable to comply with the recording jurisdiction’s recordation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: formatting requirements for an Electronic document or the jurisdiction does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: permit or provide for recordation of Electronic documents, and Sections 9206.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: or 9206.4(a) requires the Modification Agreement to be recorded or in recordable
elements.label: text

elements.text: form
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not reimburse any costs that result from a Servicer’s decision to use
elements.label: text

elements.text: an eModification Agreement, and such costs may not be assessed to the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General requirements applicable to all Freddie Mac eModification Agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Process, modify and store eModification Agreements of Freddie Mac Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: under requirements that are no less stringent than applicable industry standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: when electronically processing, modifying and storing its own Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification agreements for mortgages that it owns or services for others
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Consult with their legal counsel to ensure that the Servicer’s use, processing and
elements.label: text

elements.text: storage of an eModification Agreement complies with all applicable federal, State
elements.label: text

elements.text: and local laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Provide for Electronic notarization when required, subject to applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Comply with all requirements in the Servicing Contract to service the Mortgage,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as modified by an eModification Agreement, including, but not limited to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing obligations related to payoff or short sale (e.g., cancelation of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, Note and eModification Agreement), grant of a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, foreclosure, repurchase of an electronically modified Mortgage and
elements.label: text

elements.text: litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Assure that the signing platform has a robust audit trail of all key events starting
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the creation of the eModification Agreement through and including the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower and Servicer execution that the Servicer can reproduce upon request by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional requirements when the eModification Agreement must be recorded
elements.label: text

elements.text: or in recordable format
elements.label: text

elements.text: When an eModification Agreement must be recorded or in recordable format as
elements.label: text

elements.text: required in Sections 9206.2(c) or 9206.4(a), a Servicer may use an eModification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement, provided the Servicer is able to comply with the recording jurisdiction’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: recordation and Electronic format requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document custodial requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon execution of an eModification Agreement, the Servicer must provide a copy of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the executed eModification Agreement to its Document Custodian in a format that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable to the Document Custodian, to be maintained or logically associated with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If recordation is required, the Servicer must provide a copy of the recorded
elements.label: text

elements.text: eModification Agreement or a copy of evidence of recordation together with a copy
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the executed eModification Agreement to its Document Custodian.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Storage and safekeeping of eModification Agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: eModification Agreements (including printed paper copies of facsimiles of
elements.label: text

elements.text: eModification Agreements) must be stored in accordance with the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for storing Mortgage file documents and must be stored in an eVault or
elements.label: text

elements.text: eStorage System. eModification Agreements must be logically associated with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: paper Mortgage file documents so that all Servicing records (both paper and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic) are identified and associated with the affiliated Mortgage transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon a Transfer of Servicing involving Mortgages with eModification Agreements,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Transferor Servicer must comply with Section 7101.1(b)(ii)(B)#8 and inform the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer of the name of the eVault or eStorage System holding the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eModification Agreement. The Transferor Servicer must cause its eVault or eStorage
elements.label: text

elements.text: System provider to transfer the eModification Agreement and all related data to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer’s eVault or eStorage System provider in a manner that ensures
elements.label: text

elements.text: the ongoing validity and enforceability of the eModification Agreement and its
elements.label: text

elements.text: associated Electronic Signature. A Transferor Servicer may not satisfy its obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: under this section by relying on Section 7101.5(a) by generating paper copies of
elements.label: text

elements.text: eModification Agreements for the Transferee Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data security requirements and data privacy protection
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must follow data security requirements in Sections 1302.2 and 1401.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the data privacy protection standards in Section 8101.4(d). Servicers are required
elements.label: text

elements.text: to maintain their eStorage System and conduct periodic information security reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the data stored and maintained in such systems based on, but not limited to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable federal, State and local laws and regulations and the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to require a Servicer to implement additional security
elements.label: text

elements.text: measures regarding its Servicing eStorage System.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 1302.3 for Seller/Servicer business continuity planning
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ What is a workout Mortgage assumption?

elements.text: (a) What is a workout Mortgage assumption?
elements.label: text

elements.text: A workout Mortgage assumption permits a qualified applicant to assume title to the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Mortgage obligation from a Borrower who is currently delinquent or in imminent
elements.label: text

elements.text: danger of default on his or her Mortgage because of an eligible hardship.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This workout option has different requirements than the requirements for Transfers of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ownership allowed under certain Mortgage documents or federal law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must submit recommendations for workout Mortgage assumptions to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: for approval. Refer to Chapter 8406 for those requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) When to consider a workout Mortgage assumption
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Long-term hardships
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s eligible hardship, as described in Section 9202.1(b), is permanent or
elements.label: text

elements.text: long term and he or she cannot or does not want to retain ownership of the property but is
elements.label: text

elements.text: cooperative and has a potential buyer for the property, then the Servicer should explore
elements.label: text

elements.text: the possibility of a workout Mortgage assumption in accordance with the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 9207.1(c) through 9207.1(h) as a solution to the Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Simultaneous assumptions, relief options and modifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must also consider an assumption of the Mortgage in situations where:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All Borrowers are deceased or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ One or more Borrower(s) on the Note has an eligible hardship as described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9202.1(b) and the hardship is expected to cause a long-term or permanent decrease in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s income or increase in the Borrower’s expenses such that all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers on the Note are unable or unlikely to continue making the monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage payment obligation
elements.label: text

elements.text: In this circumstance, if a natural person with a legal or beneficial interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises wishes to assume the Mortgage obligation (“non-Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicant”), then the Servicer must first determine if the non-Borrower applicant meets
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Transfer of Ownership requirements under Chapter 8406.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the non-Borrower applicant does not meet the Transfer of Ownership requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: under Chapter 8406 and the due-on-transfer clause has been triggered or the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent, the Servicer must explore all available relief options as described in Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9203, including forbearance and payment deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined term: For purposes of this section, a natural person with a beneficial interest in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgaged Premises includes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An heir or legatee who will inherit the Mortgaged Premises following completion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: probate or distribution of the assets of the estate of the deceased Borrower; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A person awarded title to the Mortgaged Premises pursuant to a court decree or court￾approved separation agreement where a quitclaim deed has not been executed or
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorded. If a Servicer is uncertain whether a person has a beneficial interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises, it should consult its legal counsel or submit the case to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac via Resolve®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the non-Borrower applicant meets the Transfer of Ownership requirements under
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8406 and the due-on-transfer clause has not been triggered or the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent, and the Servicer determines that a relief option is unlikely to lead to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: resolution of the default or Delinquency, the non-Borrower applicant may be considered
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a simultaneous assumption and modification under the Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification® requirements. The non-Borrower applicant must provide the Servicer with
elements.label: text

elements.text: a complete Borrower Response Package, and the Servicer must evaluate the non￾Borrower applicant as if he or she were a Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may first consider whether the non-Borrower with a legal or beneficial interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the property can qualify using the non-Borrower income requirements specified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9202.1(c)(ii). If the applicant does not meet the non-Borrower income criteria in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9202.1(c)(ii) and/or wishes to assume the Mortgage obligation, then Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: must follow the requirements below to submit a recommendation to Freddie Mac for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: simultaneous assumption and modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must obtain Freddie Mac’s approval prior to offering an otherwise eligible non￾Borrower applicant a simultaneous assumption and modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To submit a recommendation for a simultaneous assumption and Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification, the Servicer must submit its recommendation to Freddie Mac via the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resolve User Interface. See Resolve Online Help for additional details. Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision will be available in the Resolve Dashboard accessible via Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Gateway®. Supporting documentation must be submitted to Freddie Mac (see Directory
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5) upon request
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac does not approve the request for a simultaneous assumption and
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification, the Servicer must refer to Section 1301.2(i) for information on adverse
elements.label: text

elements.text: action notices that must be provided to the non-Borrower applicant on behalf of Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The requirements in this Section 9207.1(b) also apply in cases where the only remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is a trust and to other transfers that require acceleration of the Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 8406 for additional information regarding Transfers of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ownership.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Eligibility requirements for a workout Mortgage assumption
elements.label: text

elements.text: To recommend a Borrower for a workout Mortgage assumption, the Servicer must complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1077, Uniform Underwriting and Transmittal Summary, and ensure that all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following eligibility requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must have an eligible hardship
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must be delinquent in his or her payments or in imminent danger of default
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must submit a complete Borrower Response Package. (See Section 9102.5
elements.label: text

elements.text: for information on the Borrower Response Package.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must be cooperative and allow access to the interior of the property for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: BPO for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Mortgage secured by a 2- to 4-unit property, a Manufactured Home or a dwelling
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to a leasehold estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Cooperative Share Loan secured by a First Lien on the Cooperative Interest to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Unit. (See Chapter 8801 for special Servicing requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The indebtedness-to-value (ITV) ratio must be equal to or greater than 85%. The total
elements.label: text

elements.text: ITV ratio is the total indebtedness under the terms of the Mortgage, which includes the
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB, accrued interest, Escrow advances and expenses (see Exhibit 57A, Approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation, Postponement Fees and Title Expenses, for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable expense limits) divided by the probable sales price determined by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The applicant assuming the Mortgage must meet Freddie Mac’s underwriting guidelines
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Topics 5100 through 5500 and Section 8406.2(a) as documented on Form 1077
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The applicant must pay a Down Payment of at least 5% of the total indebtedness unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Transfer of Ownership meets the criteria listed in Section 8406.1(c) or 8406.1(d)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Submitting a workout Mortgage assumption recommendation to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: To recommend a workout Mortgage assumption, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain a property valuation through BPOdirect® (see Section 2406.1 regarding obtaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: a property value via BPOdirect).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For a Mortgage secured by a 1-unit property (excluding a Manufactured Home, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: dwelling subject to a leasehold estate or a Cooperative Unit), the Servicer must,
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless otherwise noted below, use an available automated value
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If an automated value is not available for the Mortgage secured by a 1-unit property,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or, pursuant to Section 9207.1(c), the Borrower must be cooperative and allow access
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the interior of the property for a BPO, then the Servicer must order a new property
elements.label: text

elements.text: valuation in accordance with Sections 2406.1, 9202.4(b) and 9202.4(d), if necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The property valuation must be less than 90 days old on the date the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommends the workout Mortgage assumption to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Servicer does not need to submit the valuation to Freddie Mac because Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will have access to the value via BPOdirect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding the requirements above, if the Servicer has previously obtained a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac-compliant property valuation (i.e., Freddie Mac-provided BPO, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac-provided appraisal or an appraisal obtained in compliance with Topic 5600), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must use the Freddie Mac-compliant property valuation in connection with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout Mortgage assumption evaluation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submit all of the following to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Borrower’s complete Borrower Response Package (see Section 9102.5 for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: description of the Borrower Response Package)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Completed Form 1077 detailing the Servicer’s underwriting of the applicant
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A copy of the fully executed sales contract and addenda
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Estimated seller Closing Costs, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Mortgage is covered by mortgage insurance, a faxed copy of the approval from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the MI to Freddie Mac within two Business Days of the Servicer’s receipt of the MI’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Freddie Mac’s decision about a Servicer’s recommendation for workout Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumption
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will review the documentation the Servicer submits and make a decision to
elements.label: text

elements.text: approve or deny the workout Mortgage assumption request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Approval of request
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac approves the assumption request, it will send the Servicer an approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: letter detailing any conditions of Freddie Mac’s approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Denial of request
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac denies the assumption request, it will send the Servicer an explanation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: why Freddie Mac denied the request and provide the Servicer with the course of action it
elements.label: text

elements.text: must take to resolve the Delinquency. The Servicer must provide an adverse action notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: to all applicable parties, in addition to any other notice or disclosure required under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Equal Credit Opportunity Act, Fair Credit Reporting Act, Truth in Lending Act and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other applicable law or regulation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 1301.2(i) for more information about adverse action notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Approval conditions for a workout Mortgage assumption
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac approves the Servicer’s recommendation to allow an assumption of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, the Servicer must ensure that all conditions listed on Freddie Mac’s approval letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: are met and must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Obtain approval from the FHA, RHS, VA or MI, if applicable; for leasehold Mortgages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain the consent of the fee simple landowner/lessor to the assignment of lease, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: required under the lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Complete an Escrow analysis or establish an Escrow account to ensure that there are
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficient funds to pay the property taxes, property and mortgage insurance premiums,
elements.label: text

elements.text: etc.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Process the assumption so that the settlement occurs within 30 days of Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval and submit the assumption agreement for recordation within one Business Day
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the settlement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Per Section 1402.5(c)(ii), the Servicer must update the MERS® eRegistry (as
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined in Section 1402.1(b)) to provide notice of the assumption agreement upon a
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout Mortgage assumption (with or without a release of liability) of an eMortgage (as
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined in Section 1402.1(b)).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Comply with the documentation requirements in Section 8406.3(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Ensure that the Borrower does not receive any cash-out from the transaction unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac receives all sums due to it
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Ensure that all delinquent amounts are brought current at the time of settlement
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Retain any credit enhancement if the existing Mortgage has a credit enhancement. (If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is not the provider of the credit enhancement, it must obtain written approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the institution providing the enhancement.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. For leasehold Mortgages, ensure the transferee has acquired the transferor’s leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest either by obtaining a new lease or assuming the existing lease and that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer of the leasehold interest is recorded
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. For Cooperative Share Loans, comply with the requirements in Section 8801.3(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Closing requirements for workout Mortgage assumption
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the workout Mortgage assumption has been closed, submit the following to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac (see Directory 5) to settle the workout:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the settlement statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the executed assumption agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the deed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s contribution or promissory note, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the written approval from the provider of the credit enhancement, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Servicer fee for workout Mortgage assumption
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may charge the applicant a fee not to exceed the greater of $400 or 1% of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB of the Mortgage, to a maximum fee of $900.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9208: Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 9208.1: Short sale eligibility, Borrower requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: and approval (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ What is a short sale?
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Short sale eligibility requirements and Servicer approval authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower documentation for a short sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Short sale Borrower contributions and relocation assistance
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) What is a short sale?
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale (“short sale”) is the sale of the Mortgaged Premises for
elements.label: text

elements.text: less than the total amount necessary to satisfy the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the sale proceeds are less than the total amount due but there is a mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: claim payment or a Borrower cash contribution that results in Freddie Mac’s receiving all
elements.label: text

elements.text: sums owed on the Mortgage, then Freddie Mac considers the transaction a “make-whole”
elements.label: text

elements.text: preforeclosure sale rather than a short sale. The Servicer does not need to obtain Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s prior approval for a make-whole preforeclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9208.3(a) for Freddie Mac’s reporting and closing requirements for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: make-whole preforeclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Short sale eligibility requirements and Servicer approval authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s eligible hardship is permanent or long term and the Borrower is unable or
elements.label: text

elements.text: unwilling to sustain homeownership, then the Servicer should determine if the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: meets the eligibility requirements for a short sale. The Servicer must evaluate the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a short sale under this chapter once the Servicer has otherwise complied with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation hierarchy in Section 9201.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is subject to a recourse or indemnification agreement, the Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: approve a short sale provided the Servicer reports and Freddie Mac drafts a full payoff to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and the Servicer absorbs any losses and expenses related to the Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is an FHA, VA or Guaranteed Rural Housing Mortgage, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with the requirements of the applicable agency when approving a short sale in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: manner that ensures continued coverage of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must use the Borrower’s Delinquency status to determine eligibility,
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation requirements and Borrower contributions. The Servicer determines the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency status at the time of evaluation for eligibility and Borrower contribution in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When determining eligibility in accordance with this section, the Servicer must use the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency status of the Mortgage on the date the Servicer begins the evaluation using
elements.label: text

elements.text: required documentation pursuant to Section 9208.1(c)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the Servicer’s evaluation for eligibility did not include a review of a purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: offer, the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a contribution, if applicable, once the
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase offer is received. The Servicer must determine the Delinquency status of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage when beginning the review of the purchase offer. If the credit report is now
elements.label: text

elements.text: greater than 90 days old from the date of initial evaluation for eligibility, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must order a new credit report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Eligibility requirements for a short sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: Every Borrower, regardless of Delinquency status, is eligible to be considered for a short
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale, provided the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers who do not meet the requirements for a Streamlined Short Sale (as defined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 9208.1(c)(i)(A)) must be experiencing or have experienced one of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible hardships listed in Section 9202.1(b)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The sale must be an arm’s length transaction as defined in Section 9208.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower has listed the Mortgaged Premises for sale with a licensed real estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: broker who in turn must have listed the Mortgaged Premises on the Multiple Listing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Service (MLS) covering the market in which the Mortgaged Premises is located for at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least five consecutive days. The listing requirements are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The listing period must include at least one weekend (i.e., Saturday and Sunday)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The listing must be in an “active” status for at least the five days immediately
elements.label: text

elements.text: preceding the day on which the purchase offer is accepted by the Borrower and
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted to the Servicer for approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Mortgaged Premises is located in an area that is not covered by an MLS,
elements.label: text

elements.text: then it must be advertised for sale by the real estate broker in a manner customary
elements.label: text

elements.text: for that real estate market at least five consecutive calendar days, which must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include at least one weekend
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer must retain a copy of the MLS listing of the Mortgaged Premises in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage file (or documentation of the advertisement(s) if the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is located in an area not covered by an MLS). The Borrower may not act
elements.label: text

elements.text: as the listing agent or attempt to sell the Mortgaged Premises without a licensed
elements.label: text

elements.text: real estate professional.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower has not acquired a new Mortgage in the six months preceding the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Delinquency or, if the Borrower is current, in the six months preceding
elements.label: text

elements.text: the evaluation of the Borrower for a short sale. The Borrower is only permitted to
elements.label: text

elements.text: have obtained a new Mortgage if the Borrower’s eligible hardship was distant
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must not have entered into a program or arrangement where a third
elements.label: text

elements.text: party takes title to the Mortgaged Premises and arranges a short sale in exchange for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower who is current or less than 60 days delinquent must meet the imminent
elements.label: text

elements.text: default requirements as described in Section 9208.1(c)(iii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Servicer approval authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise notified by Freddie Mac, all Servicers are delegated to approve a short
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale that meets the eligibility requirements of Section 9208.1(b)(ii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional circumstances servicers are not delegated to approve short sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Short sales Section 8801.3(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower documentation for a short sale Section 9208.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Short sale Borrower contributions and relocation assistance Section 9208.1(d)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Property valuation and minimum net proceeds for short sales Section 9208.2(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Communication timelines for short sales Section 9208.2(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Short sale transactions and processing requirements Section 9208.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Submitting a short sale recommendation to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: For situations where the Borrower does not meet the eligibility requirements for a short
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale and the Servicer determines a short sale may be the best option for addressing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency or imminent Delinquency, the Servicer must use Resolve® to submit a
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommendation to Freddie Mac for review with the following required documentation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Complete Borrower Response Package or other documentation as permitted in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9208.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. A copy of the fully executed sales contract on the Mortgaged Premises, with addenda,
elements.label: text

elements.text: stating that it is being purchased in “as-is” condition. If the buyer obtains FHA, RHS
elements.label: text

elements.text: or VA financing, then the contract does not need to include the “as-is” condition if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA, RHS or VA requires the condition to be removed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Preliminary Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. For Mortgages secured by properties subject to resale restrictions (in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4406, 4502 or 4504, as applicable), the applicable resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: containing details on the property valuation and/or the restricted resale price as well
elements.label: text

elements.text: as any other terms and conditions that must be met
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. For Cooperative Share Loans, the additional documentation listed in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the Servicer may submit a recommendation to Freddie Mac for consideration
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the Borrower does not have an eligible hardship but one of the following conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: exists and, in the Servicer’s judgment, the short sale is an appropriate resolution to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Borrower’s Credit Score requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower’s Credit Score must be no more than 90 days old as of the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer evaluates the Borrower for a short sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there is more than one Borrower on the Mortgage, the Servicer must choose one
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Score that is adequately indicative of the credit reputation of all Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: currently on the Mortgage. The following method must be used:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Servicer must first select a single Credit Score for each Borrower on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. If the Servicer obtains multiple Credit Scores for a single Borrower, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must use the middle/lower method to select the single Credit Score for that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. If there are multiple Borrowers on the Mortgage, the Servicer must determine the
elements.label: text

elements.text: single Credit Score for each Borrower using the method described above
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Servicer must then select either the lowest Credit Score across all Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the Mortgage or the average Credit Score from all Borrowers’ single scores.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Whichever method is used, the Servicer must choose the single Credit Score using the
elements.label: text

elements.text: same method and procedure for all Borrowers and for all Mortgages consistent with
elements.label: text

elements.text: fair lending laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower was previously determined to be eligible for a Streamlined Short Sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: and had yet to receive a purchase offer, then when a subsequent purchase offer is
elements.label: text

elements.text: received, the Servicer may use the same Credit Score obtained for the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: initial evaluation of the Borrower for a short sale even if the Credit Score has become

elements.text: (ii) Evaluating the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Evaluating the credit report
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain a credit report for each Borrower on the Mortgage (or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: joint report for co-Borrowers). The credit report must be no more than 90 days old as
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the date the Servicer evaluates the Borrower for a short sale. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: review the credit report to verify that the Borrower meets the relevant requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a short sale and to evaluate the Borrower’s ability to make a contribution.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (I) Verifying occupancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Borrowers who are current or less than 60 days delinquent, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: review the credit report to verify that at least one of the Borrowers is occupying
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgaged Premises as a Primary Residence. If the credit report does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicate that the Mortgaged Premises is the Primary Residence for at least one
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, then the Servicer must use good business judgment in reconciling the
elements.label: text

elements.text: inconsistency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (II)New Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless a Borrower is eligible for a Streamlined Short Sale, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: review the Borrower’s credit report to determine whether the Borrower obtained a
elements.label: text

elements.text: new Mortgage(s) in the six months preceding the Borrower’s Delinquency or, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower is current, in the six months preceding the evaluation of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower for a short sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer’s review of the credit report reveals a new Mortgage, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may approve the short sale only if the hardship was due to distant employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer, relocation due to new employment or Permanent Change of Station
elements.label: text

elements.text: (PCS) orders and the Servicer verifies that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower intends to occupy the property securing the new Mortgage as
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The new employment location is greater than 50 miles one-way from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The new property address is reasonably near the Borrower’s new employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: location
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has any other hardship and the Servicer determines a new
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage has been obtained, the Servicer is not delegated to approve the short
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale and must submit the request to Freddie Mac for consideration.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer’s review of the credit report indicates that a mortgage creditor has
elements.label: text

elements.text: made an inquiry within the previous four-month period, the Servicer must contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower to determine the following on the Mortgage sought:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The address of the property,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The purpose of the inquiry (e.g., refinance or purchase Mortgage), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The result of the inquiry (e.g., refinance or purchase Mortgage is pending,
elements.label: text

elements.text: closed or canceled)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a purchase Mortgage was obtained, then the Servicer is not delegated to
elements.label: text

elements.text: approve the short sale, and the file must be sent to Freddie Mac. Refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9208.1(b) regarding submission of a short sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Special requirements for Borrowers who are current or less than 60 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower is current or less than 60 days delinquent at the time of initial evaluation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: he or she must be considered to be in non-retention imminent default following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: business rules in the chart below unless the Borrower was discharged from a Chapter 7
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy in order to be eligible for a short sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Non-retention Imminent default evaluation business rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resolve will evaluate the information the Servicer provides against the imminent
elements.label: text

elements.text: default business rules. Any Borrower who is current or less than 60 days delinquent at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the time the Servicer begins the initial evaluation is in imminent default if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower meets the requirements of the following business rules:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Non-retention imminent default evaluation business rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be considered in non-retention imminent default, the Borrower must meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: all requirements under business rule 1 and must meet the requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 2, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 3
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business rule
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Borrower must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submit a complete Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be current or less than 60 days delinquent (i.e., less than three
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payments past due) on the Mortgage as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Occupy the property as a Primary Residence (or at least one
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower on the Mortgage must occupy the property as his or
elements.label: text

elements.text: her Primary Residence)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Non-retention imminent default evaluation business rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be considered in non-retention imminent default, the Borrower must meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: all requirements under business rule 1 and must meet the requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 2, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 3
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have Cash Reserves (as defined in Section 9208.1(c)(iii)(C)(I))
elements.label: text

elements.text: less than $25,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have an eligible hardship as described in Section 9202.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Requirements related to occupancy and non-retirement liquid
elements.label: text

elements.text: assets do not apply if the Borrower is a Servicemember with PCS
elements.label: text

elements.text: orders and the property securing the Mortgage is or was the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Primary Residence where the transfer or new
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment location is greater than 50 miles one way from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property securing the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business rule
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower is considered in non-retention imminent default if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower meets the requirements of business rule 1, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s Credit Score is less than or equal to 620
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined in accordance with Section 9206.1(e)(v); AND
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage has had two or more 30-day Delinquencies in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: most recent 6-month period; OR
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s housing expense-to-income ratio is greater than
elements.label: text

elements.text: 40% as of the evaluation date
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has one of the Imminent Default Hardships
elements.label: text

elements.text: described below in business rule 3, the Borrower may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined to be in imminent default even if these business rule 2
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements are not met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business rule
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower is considered in non-retention imminent default if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower meets the requirements of business rule 1, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower provided the documentation required in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9202.1(b) supporting one of the Imminent Default Hardships listed
elements.label: text

elements.text: below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Death of a Borrower or death of either the primary or secondary
elements.label: text

elements.text: wage earner in the household
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Long-term or permanent disability or serious illness of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower/co-Borrower or dependent family member
elements.label: text

elements.text: Non-retention imminent default evaluation business rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be considered in non-retention imminent default, the Borrower must meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: all requirements under business rule 1 and must meet the requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 2, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 3
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Divorce or legal separation or separation of Borrower unrelated
elements.label: text

elements.text: by marriage, civil union or similar domestic partnership under
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Distant employment transfer or relocation due to new
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment or PCS orders where the property securing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage being evaluated is the Borrower’s Primary Residence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The new employment location must be more than 50 miles one
elements.label: text

elements.text: way from the property securing the Mortgage being evaluated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Imminent Default Hardship must currently cause and be
elements.label: text

elements.text: expected to continue to cause a long-term or permanent decrease in
elements.label: text

elements.text: income or increase in expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must always submit all information for business rule 1 and business rule
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2, even if the Borrower does not meet the requirements under business rule 2 and
elements.label: text

elements.text: instead is approved based on the Imminent Default Hardship under business rule 3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B)Income and asset documentation and verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: (I) Documentation verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be evaluated for non-retention imminent default, a Borrower must, at a
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum, provide a complete Borrower Response Package as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9102.5(c). In addition to the income documentation required under Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9202.1(c), the Servicer must obtain the Borrower’s Credit Score in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Section 9208.1(c)(iii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (II)Verification of income and assets; resolution of material inconsistencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must review all documentation submitted by the Borrower to identify
elements.label: text

elements.text: any material inconsistencies, including material inconsistencies with a tax return
elements.label: text

elements.text: or tax transcript if one was obtained under Section 9202.1(c). If, based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s good business judgment, there are material inconsistencies with respect
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the income or asset information disclosed by the Borrower or with other
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation relevant to the imminent default decision, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Obtain other documentation to reasonably reconcile such material
elements.label: text

elements.text: inconsistencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Document such material differences in their servicing system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer cannot reconcile such material differences, the Borrower cannot be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered in imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C)Cash Reserves test
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete an evaluation of the Borrower’s Cash Reserves. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower must have Cash Reserves of less than $25,000 to be further evaluated for
elements.label: text

elements.text: imminent default. If the Borrower either discloses or provides documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicating the Borrower has Cash Reserves equal to or greater than $25,000, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is not in imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (I) Definition of Cash Reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of determining imminent default, Cash Reserves are defined as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash Reserves: Any non-retirement liquid asset the Borrower has available for
elements.label: text

elements.text: withdrawal from any financial institution or brokerage, including funds on deposit
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Borrower’s checking, savings, money market or certificate of deposit
elements.label: text

elements.text: account or other depository account, stocks, bonds, mutual funds, U.S.
elements.label: text

elements.text: government securities and other securities that are traded on an exchange or
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketplace generally available to the public (e.g., New York Stock Exchange,
elements.label: text

elements.text: National Association of Securities Dealers Automated Quotations, Midwest SE,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chicago Board of Trade or Over the Counter) for which the price can be readily
elements.label: text

elements.text: verified through financial publications.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assets are only considered retirement assets if they are held in a qualified
elements.label: text

elements.text: retirement account such as a 401k, 403b, 457, Individual Retirement Account or
elements.label: text

elements.text: pension fund. If the assets are not held in a retirement account, the assets must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered Cash Reserves.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (II)Calculating Cash Reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must calculate the Borrower’s Cash Reserves in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Servicer must determine that, for every Borrower on the Mortgage, all of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s Cash Reserves have been accounted for on Form 710,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Assistance Application
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. In making the determination that all Cash Reserves have been accounted for,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must review all information provided by the Borrower to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine if the asset information stated on Form 710 is reasonably consistent
elements.label: text

elements.text: with information available from all other information provided by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, including verbal information shared by the Borrower. If there are
elements.label: text

elements.text: inconsistencies between the Borrower’s disclosure of assets and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information provided by the Borrower, then the Servicer must obtain the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s tax return or tax transcript in order to reconcile the
elements.label: text

elements.text: inconsistencies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, upon reviewing the Borrower’s tax return or tax transcript, if applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer observes interest, dividend income or gains/losses that, in total,
elements.label: text

elements.text: could not be reasonably produced by the Borrower’s disclosed Cash Reserves,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and such income indicates deposits, securities holdings or other assets could
elements.label: text

elements.text: be in excess of the amounts disclosed by the Borrower on Form 710, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must reconcile the inconsistency with the Borrower. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must require the Borrower to produce a signed federal tax return and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: relevant schedules, in the event the Servicer used a tax transcript in lieu of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: tax return, along with any other relevant documentation that verifies the
elements.label: text

elements.text: disposition and/or current status of those assets, which produced the income or
elements.label: text

elements.text: gains/losses to resolve the inconsistency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure that the Borrower’s disclosure of assets is
elements.label: text

elements.text: reasonably accurate despite the inconsistency between the disclosed assets and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the income or gain/loss from assets reported on the tax return or tax transcript.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In determining what documentation is needed to reconcile an inconsistency,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must review the detailed tax return schedules and forms and
elements.label: text

elements.text: request from the Borrower copies of recent and past statements from those
elements.label: text

elements.text: asset holdings or transactions indicated on the schedules and forms that
elements.label: text

elements.text: produced the income or gain/loss (e.g., checking, savings, brokerage account
elements.label: text

elements.text: statements, asset sale statements or records).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. If there are inconsistencies between the Borrower’s disclosure of assets and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the tax return information that cannot be reconciled, the Borrower cannot be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered in imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that the Borrower has Cash Reserves of less than
elements.label: text

elements.text: $25,000 and meets all other requirements of Section 9208.1(c)(iii), then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is considered to be in imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (D)Imminent Default Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must choose one Credit Score that is adequately indicative of the credit
elements.label: text
