elements.text: reputation of all Borrowers currently on the Mortgage. Servicers must use a Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Score based on the credit-scoring model. This score must be obtained and determined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with the requirements below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (I) Obtaining Credit Scores for each Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must request a Credit Score for each Borrower on the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: from any one of the following three credit repositories:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Equifax Credit Information Services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Experian Information Systems and Services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ TransUnion Credit Information Company
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower’s Credit Score must be less than 90 days old on the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer performs the imminent default evaluation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (II)Borrowers with no available Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: It is unusual for any Borrower who has obtained a Mortgage not to have a Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Score. If no single Credit Score can be identified for a Borrower, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must recheck the information provided when ordering the Credit Scores and
elements.label: text

elements.text: resubmit a request. If the Servicer is still unable to obtain a Credit Score for that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, it may rely on the Credit Scores of all other Borrowers as determined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Absent a Credit Score for any Borrower on the Mortgage, the Borrower may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: be determined to be in imminent default under the requirements of business rule
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2, and the Servicer must proceed to evaluate the Borrower under the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of business rule 3 in Section 9208.1(c)(i). In such instances when a Credit Score is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not available for any Borrower on the Mortgage, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Maintain documentation in the Mortgage file that demonstrates the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: attempts to obtain Credit Scores from all three credit repositories on all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Enter the result that a Credit Score is not available for any Borrower on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage into Resolve
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Proceed to the Imminent Default Hardship test in business rule 3 to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: if an Imminent Default Hardship exists
elements.label: text

elements.text: (III)Determining the Imminent Default Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must identify the Imminent Default Credit Score in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer must first select a single Credit Score for each Borrower on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage. If the Servicer obtains multiple Credit Scores for a single
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, the Servicer must use the middle/lower method to select the single
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Score for that Borrower. This method is the most predictive when
elements.label: text

elements.text: determining a single Borrower’s overall credit reputation. If three Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Scores are obtained for a Borrower, the single score for that Borrower is the
elements.label: text

elements.text: one with the middle value. For example, if the Credit Scores were 660, 656
elements.label: text

elements.text: and 640, the single Credit Score selected by the Servicer should be 656. When
elements.label: text

elements.text: there is a duplicate score, the Seller must select that score to be the single
elements.label: text

elements.text: score. If the Credit Scores for a Borrower were 660, 660 and 640, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: should select 660. If two Credit Scores were obtained for a Borrower, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must select the lower of the two Credit Scores to be the single Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Score for that Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If there is only one Borrower on the Mortgage, the single Credit Score,
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined in accordance with the above requirements, is considered the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Imminent Default Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If there are multiple Borrowers on the Mortgage, the Servicer must determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: the single Credit Score for each Borrower using the method described above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must then select either the lowest Credit Score across all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers on the Mortgage or the average Credit Score from all Borrowers’
elements.label: text

elements.text: single scores. (Note: Whichever method is used, the Servicer should choose
elements.label: text

elements.text: the single Credit Score using the same method and procedure for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers and for all Mortgages consistent with fair lending laws.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (E) Calculating housing expense-to-income (HTI) ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must input verified income data into Resolve. Based on the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: input, Resolve will determine if the Borrower’s HTI ratio is greater than 40%. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes of this determination, the Borrower’s current monthly housing expense is
elements.label: text

elements.text: divided by the Borrower’s monthly gross income (or the Borrowers’ combined
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly gross income in the case of co-Borrowers) plus any allowable non-obligor
elements.label: text

elements.text: household income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower’s current monthly housing expense consists of the following, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly principal and interest payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly pro rata amount for real estate taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly pro rata amount for property or flood insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly pro rata amount for homeowners association (HOA) dues, Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unit or Cooperative Unit Maintenance Fees and ground rent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any escrow shortage currently included as part of the monthly contractual
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has been granted interest rate relief under the Servicemembers Civil
elements.label: text

elements.text: Relief Act (SCRA), the Servicer must use the principal and interest payment and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: contractual rate of interest in effect on the Note prior to the granting of the SCRA
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief rather than the temporarily SCRA-reduced interest rate and related SCRA
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payment when calculating the Borrower’s current monthly HTI ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower has indicated that there are condominium/HOA or Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation assessments (see Chapter 8801 for special Servicing requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans), Condominium Unit maintenance fees or Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Maintenance Fees or ground rents but has not been able to provide written
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation to verify these amounts, the Servicer must rely on the information
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided by the Borrower if the Servicer has made reasonable efforts to obtain the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts in writing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The current monthly housing expense does not include mortgage insurance premium
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments or payments due to holders of subordinate liens.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on the information provided by the Servicer, Resolve will calculate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s HTI ratio and determine if it meets the imminent default requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: under business rule 2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (F) Payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on the information provided by the Servicer, Resolve will review the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s payment history and determine if it meets the imminent default
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements under business rule 2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (G)Imminent default evaluation results
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower meets the requirements of business rule 1 and meets the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of either business rule 2 or business rule 3, the Borrower is in imminent default. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a short sale and no further analysis is
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by the Servicer to determine imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (H)General requirements and information
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that a Borrower is in imminent default, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue evaluating the Borrower using the applicable underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: outlined in this chapter to determine if the Borrower qualifies for a short sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Short sale Borrower contributions and relocation assistance
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Borrower contributions towards the deficiency
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that the Borrower’s Cash Reserves exceed $10,000 or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s HTI ratio is less than or equal to 40%, the Servicer must request a cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: contribution in accordance with the requirements below. The Servicer must verbally
elements.label: text

elements.text: confirm the assets reported on Form 710 and reconcile any differences with
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation following the procedure in Section 9202.1(c). A Servicer may negotiate
elements.label: text

elements.text: contribution amounts less than the initial contribution requests, which must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined in accordance with the cash contribution formula in this section. When a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer negotiates a contribution that is less than the initial request, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: document the reason for its decision(s) in the Mortgage file and note the specific financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances that limit the Borrower’s ability to contribute towards the deficiency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless Freddie Mac has delegated authority with the MI or communicates otherwise, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage is covered by mortgage insurance and the MI requires a contribution from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower that is greater than the contribution limits required by this section in order
elements.label: text

elements.text: to approve the short sale, the Servicer must require the Borrower to make the contribution
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by the MI as a condition of approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers are not required to make a contribution in the following instances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers who are service members with PCS orders, provided the property securing
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage is or was previously the Borrower’s Primary Residence where the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer or new employment location is greater than 50 miles one way from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property securing the Mortgage being evaluated
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers who qualify for a Streamlined Short Sale (refer to Section 9208.1(c))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Applicable law prohibits requesting or receiving a contribution
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s Cash Reserves exceed $10,000 or his or her HTI ratio is less than or
elements.label: text

elements.text: equal to 40%, the Servicer must request the greater of 20% of the Cash Reserves or four
elements.label: text

elements.text: times principal, interest, taxes and insurance as a cash contribution. The cash contribution
elements.label: text

elements.text: must not exceed the total amount of the deficiency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Borrowers who are current or less than 60 days delinquent and meet the criteria
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in the Cash Reserves test
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower is current or less than 60 days delinquent and meets the criteria
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in in the Cash Reserves test, the Borrower must contribute a minimum of
elements.label: text

elements.text: 20% of their Cash Reserves. If the Borrower is unwilling to contribute 20% of their
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash Reserves, the Servicer must submit the case to Freddie Mac for review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Borrowers who are 60 or more days delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower who is 60 or more days delinquent cannot contribute 20% of his or her
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash Reserves, the Servicer may negotiate a lower level of contribution. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer negotiates and collects less than 20% of the Cash Reserves, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must document the specific circumstances that limit the Borrower’s ability to make
elements.label: text

elements.text: the initially requested financial level of cash contribution (i.e., 20% of the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash Reserves) in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on the Servicer’s assessment of the Borrower’s written or stated ability to pay in
elements.label: text

elements.text: combination with its evaluation of the Borrower’s financial and hardship information, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is authorized to negotiate a lower contribution amount. Additionally, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may determine that the Borrower’s individual circumstances warrant a lower
elements.label: text

elements.text: starting point to cash contribution negotiations or no contribution. If a Borrower refuses
elements.label: text

elements.text: to contribute an amount the Servicer deems acceptable, then the short sale is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: delegated and must be submitted to Freddie Mac following the process in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9208.1(b). If the Servicer determines the Borrower is unable to contribute at least $500
elements.label: text

elements.text: toward the deficiency, then the Servicer must not collect a cash contribution.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s Cash Reserves are in excess of $50,000, the Servicer must submit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: short sale request to Freddie Mac for review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 9208.1(b) regarding the details for submitting a short sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommendation to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Borrower relocation assistance
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that the Borrower is not required to make a financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: contribution toward the deficiency, the Borrower is eligible to receive up to $7,500 in
elements.label: text

elements.text: relocation assistance provided that the Borrower occupies the Mortgaged Premises as his
elements.label: text

elements.text: or her Primary Residence. The Borrower is not eligible to receive relocation assistance in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following circumstances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower will receive relocation assistance from a source other than Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: or the Servicer, such as an employer, and the amount is equal to or greater than
elements.label: text

elements.text: $7,500. If the Borrower will receive relocation assistance from a source other than
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac or the Servicer and the amount is less than $7,500, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: reduce the amount of Freddie Mac’s relocation assistance by the amount received
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the other source.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer determines that the Borrower’s Cash Reserves exceed $10,000 or his or
elements.label: text

elements.text: her HTI ratio is less than or equal to 40%, regardless of whether the Borrower makes
elements.label: text

elements.text: a contribution. If the Servicer believes such a Borrower is experiencing financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances necessitating relocation assistance, the Servicer may submit the file to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac. Refer to Section 9208.1(b) regarding the details for submission to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower is subject to PCS orders and receives government assistance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: relocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If the Borrower receives $1,000 of relocation assistance from his or her
elements.label: text

elements.text: employer, Freddie Mac may provide the Borrower with an additional $6,500 of relocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must instruct the settlement agent to pay the Freddie Mac relocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance from sale proceeds and itemize it and any relocation assistance received from
elements.label: text

elements.text: other entities separately on the Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement to the extent that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer is aware of other relocation or transition assistance payments being paid as
elements.label: text

elements.text: part of the short sale transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the relocation assistance offered by Freddie Mac, the Servicer may in its
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion offer the Borrower an additional financial incentive to complete the short sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any relocation assistance provided by the Servicer will be in addition to the relocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance amount provided by Freddie Mac and the Servicer’s assistance amount does
elements.label: text

elements.text: not have to be subtracted from the assistance amount Freddie Mac will pay. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer does offer its own financial assistance, it must not deduct this payment from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds of the short sale and must provide the incentive from its own funds, either by
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment made directly to the Borrower, or through the settlement agent closing the short
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale transaction. Payments made to the Borrower by the Servicer must be reflected on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement in accordance with applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The relocation assistance payment may not be applied to other debts secured by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9208.2: Property valuation, communications,
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: processing and transaction management for short
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property valuation and minimum net proceeds for short sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Communication timelines for short sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Short sale transaction and processing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Property valuation and minimum net proceeds for short sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) When to obtain a property valuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer has evaluated the Borrower for all other alternatives to foreclosure in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Freddie Mac’s evaluation hierarchy set forth in Section 9201.2 and
elements.label: text

elements.text: believes that the Borrower meets the eligibility requirements for a Freddie Mac Standard
elements.label: text

elements.text: Short Sale (“short sale”), then, unless otherwise instructed by Freddie Mac, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must obtain an interior property valuation in accordance with the Guide, if the Borrower:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Has listed the Mortgaged Premises for sale; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Has expressed interest in a short sale; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Notifies the Servicer of a purchase offer on the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain a short sale property value from Freddie Mac, as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: when one of the above situations occurs. An evaluation for a Borrower contribution
elements.label: text

elements.text: should not delay the Servicer’s request for a property valuation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) How to obtain a short sale property value and minimum net proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: With the exception of Mortgages secured by properties subject to resale restrictions (in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Chapter 4406, 4502 or 4504, as applicable), the Servicer must submit a
elements.label: text

elements.text: request to Freddie Mac for the short sale property value and the minimum net proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: via the “Obtain Valuation” tab in Freddie Mac Real Estate Valuation and Pricing tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: when considering a Borrower for a short sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must advise the Borrower that the person evaluating the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises must be given interior access and that the Borrower must otherwise cooperate
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the inspection.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An “estimated market value” of the Mortgaged Premises and the “minimum net
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds” as determined by Freddie Mac will be returned by the Real Estate Valuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Pricing tool (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) with a “good through date” indicating
elements.label: text

elements.text: the expiration date of the property value and minimum net proceeds amount. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is unable to render an evaluation decision on a purchase offer prior to the good
elements.label: text

elements.text: through date, a new property value and minimum net proceeds must be obtained via the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Real Estate Valuation and Pricing tool to evaluate the purchase offer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Chapter 2404 for terms and requirements for using Freddie Mac’s Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tools.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Servicer enters a short sale into Resolve® without obtaining a property value
elements.label: text

elements.text: or minimum net proceeds or if those values have expired, Resolve will obtain the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property value and minimum net proceeds automatically.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Listing price guidance
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a short sale property value and minimum net proceeds were obtained, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: should provide listing price guidance to the Borrower or real estate broker based upon the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property value obtained from Freddie Mac. However, the Servicer must inform the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower or real estate broker of the following when providing such guidance:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The value provided is only guidance. Such guidance should not be presented as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: required listing price to the Borrower or real estate broker. The Borrower and the real
elements.label: text

elements.text: estate broker are responsible for determining the list price for the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All transactions must meet the minimum net proceeds required by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: regardless of the value provided. An offer at or above the value provided by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may not necessarily result in an acceptable level of net proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to providing listing price guidance, Servicers must communicate a list of
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable closing expense categories to the Borrower and the real estate broker as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 9208.2(c)(iv).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Mortgages secured by properties subject to resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are not required to obtain short sale property values and minimum net proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: on Mortgages secured by properties subject to with resale restrictions (in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4406, 4502 or 4504, as applicable). If the Servicer is considering a Borrower for
elements.label: text

elements.text: a short sale on a Mortgage secured by a property subject to resale restrictions, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is not delegated to approve the short sale, and the file must be sent to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac. Refer to Section 9208.1(b) regarding submission of a short sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For listing price guidance and acceptable closing expense categories that may be specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Mortgages secured by properties subject to resale restrictions, Servicers must refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the applicable resale restrictions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Communication timelines for short sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following chart sets forth the required response times when a short sale is being
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered as a solution to the Delinquency for either a Mortgage that is not secured by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary Residence or a Mortgage that is secured by a Primary Residence and the Servicer is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not evaluating the Borrower for a short sale using the First Complete Borrower Response
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package received more than 37 days prior to a scheduled foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Short sale: Submission of purchase offer and Servicer decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days Action required
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5 Business Days The Servicer must acknowledge receipt of the purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: offer within five Business Days.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the purchase offer is submitted with insufficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: information, the Servicer must notify the Borrower of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information needed to evaluate the offer within five
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Days of receipt of the offer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Short sale: Submission of purchase offer and Servicer decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days Action required
elements.label: text

elements.text: 30 days The Servicer must respond to the Borrower with a purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision within 30 days of receipt of the purchase offer. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the purchase offer does not meet Freddie Mac’s minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: net proceeds threshold and/or is denied by Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must notify the Borrower and include an
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable counteroffer in its response.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: In the event that a Borrower has submitted a
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete Borrower Response Package and a purchase offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: simultaneously, the response period for evaluating both the
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete Borrower Response Package and the purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: offer is the same as outlined in this section (i.e., provide a
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision within 30 days following receipt of purchase offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Borrower Response Package).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Short sale: Counteroffers (if purchase offer is less than the minimum net
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days Action required
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5 Business Days Borrower must respond to the Servicer’s counteroffer
elements.label: text

elements.text: within five Business Days of the date the counteroffer is
elements.label: text

elements.text: received.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10 Business Days Servicer must respond to the Borrower within 10 Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days of receipt of the Borrower’s response to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: counteroffer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain documentation of all communications to and from the Borrower,
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether verbal or written, and including status updates, either in the Mortgage file or in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s Servicing system. In addition, the Servicer must provide the information to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac for review upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 9101.3 for foreclosure suspension requirements when the First Complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Response Package is received more than 37 days prior to a scheduled foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale. Refer to Sections 9102.5(c) and 9301.7(a) for foreclosure suspension requirements for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage that has been referred to foreclosure and is either not secured by a Primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residence or secured by a Primary Residence and the Servicer is not evaluating the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a short sale using the First Complete Borrower Response Package received more than 37
elements.label: text

elements.text: days prior to a scheduled foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Short sale transaction and processing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order for the short sale to be approved by either the Servicer or Freddie Mac, the short sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction must meet the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Sales proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must review the executed sales contract and preliminary Settlement/Closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disclosure Statement to ensure the minimum net proceeds amount provided by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac via the Freddie Mac Real Estate Valuation and Pricing tool, if applicable, will result
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the sale unless otherwise permitted by Freddie Mac in writing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must not receive any proceeds from the sale of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: other than relocation assistance paid to the Borrower in accordance with the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Section 9208.1(d) and reflected on the Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Determining if the transaction meets the minimum net proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: If applicable, when determining whether the transaction meets or exceeds the minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: net proceeds as provided by the Real Estate Valuation and Pricing tool, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: deduct from the gross sales price the following allowable transaction costs up to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum amount as described below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Allowable Closing Costs reasonable and customary for the jurisdiction where the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Premises is located, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Property taxes and other assessments prorated to the date of closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Typical and customary local and state transfer taxes and stamps
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Title and settlement charges typically paid by the seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Seller’s attorney fees for settlement services typically provided by a title or
elements.label: text

elements.text: escrow company
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Wood-destroying pest inspections and treatment, when required by local law or
elements.label: text

elements.text: custom
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Past-due condominium/homeowners association (HOA) or Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation assessments (see Chapter 8801 for special Servicing requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans), Condominium Unit maintenance fees or Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unit Maintenance Fees and ground rents, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The following transaction costs are subject to more specific parameters, as indicated
elements.label: text

elements.text: below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Real estate broker commission, including any short sale negotiation fees, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, as described in Sections 9208.2(c)(v) and 9208.2(c)(vi)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Borrower incentives (if applicable), as described in Section 9208.1(d)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Payment to subordinate mortgage holders (if applicable), as provided in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any other amounts authorized by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The following transaction costs are prohibited by Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Real estate sales commissions paid to the Borrower or the purchaser
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Buyer’s discount points or mortgage loan origination costs; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Fees that are not usual or customary to the local market
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower contributions must not be counted toward the minimum net proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If the purchase offer submitted by the Borrower/real estate broker is $100,000,
elements.label: text

elements.text: then the Servicer will determine whether the minimum net proceeds are met as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: $100,000 (Gross sales price)
elements.label: text

elements.text: -$9,000 (Allowable Closing Costs including real estate commission)
elements.label: text

elements.text: -$6,000 (Payment to subordinate mortgage holders)
elements.label: text

elements.text: -$7,500 (Borrower relocation assistance)
elements.label: text

elements.text: $77,500 (Determine whether this amount meets or exceeds the minimum net
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds provided in the Real Estate Valuation and Pricing tool)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not disclose the minimum net proceeds to any party, if applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to, the Borrower, the real estate broker and any prospective
elements.label: text

elements.text: buyer, except as authorized by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As applicable, the Servicer must ensure that the transaction meets or exceeds the
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum net proceeds amount prior to approving a short sale transaction. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum net proceeds are not met, the Servicer must provide a counteroffer for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount that would meet the minimum net proceeds in accordance with the response time
elements.label: text

elements.text: frames set forth in Section 9208.2(b). Under no circumstances is the Servicer delegated to
elements.label: text

elements.text: approve a transaction if the amount to be remitted to Freddie Mac at closing is less than
elements.label: text

elements.text: the minimum net proceeds amount, if applicable. If the transaction does not meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum net proceeds established by Freddie Mac, the Servicer may submit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage to Freddie Mac for further review. (Refer to Section 9208.1(b) for more
elements.label: text

elements.text: information regarding the requirements for submitting a recommendation to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Payments to subordinate mortgage holders
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each short sale completed in accordance with Sections 9208.1 through 9208.3(a), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may authorize the settlement agent closing the short sale transaction to pay
elements.label: text

elements.text: subordinate mortgage holders an aggregate amount of six thousand dollars ($6,000.00)
elements.label: text

elements.text: from sales proceeds at the same time that all other payments, including the payoff to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, are disbursed by the settlement agent. The subordinate mortgage holders
elements.label: text

elements.text: may not receive any other payments, in cash, promissory note or otherwise, from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower in connection with approval of the short sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there are multiple subordinate mortgage holders, the Servicer has the discretion to
elements.label: text

elements.text: divide the subordinate mortgage payment among them so as to maximize the chances that
elements.label: text

elements.text: all subordinate mortgage holders will approve the short sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment of any amount to subordinate mortgage holders is contingent upon agreement by
elements.label: text

elements.text: all mortgage holders to release their Mortgages and, if they are accepting a payment from
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, extinguish the indebtedness secured by the Mortgaged Premises. In
elements.label: text

elements.text: addition, subordinate mortgage holders accepting payment from Freddie Mac must agree
elements.label: text

elements.text: in writing to waive all rights to seek a deficiency judgment against the Borrower. If a
elements.label: text

elements.text: subordinate mortgage holder releases its Mortgage to allow the short sale to close but
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not extinguish the indebtedness, the mortgage holder will not receive a payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: from Freddie Mac. Regardless of whether payment is made to a subordinate mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: holder, the Servicer must obtain written commitment from the subordinate mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: holder(s) to release the Mortgage(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: All payments made to subordinate mortgage holders must be documented on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement in accordance with applicable law. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must have established written policies governing how subordinate mortgage payments are
elements.label: text

elements.text: paid and the Servicer must provide evidence to Borrowers that the subordinate mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: holder has agreed to accept the payment, extinguish the secured indebtedness and waive
elements.label: text

elements.text: all rights to seek a deficiency judgment against the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Only Mortgages, deeds of trust or security deeds recorded in the land records and
elements.label: text

elements.text: constituting a valid lien against the property are eligible for a payment from Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any and all other types of liens, including, but not limited to, judgments, mechanic’s and
elements.label: text

elements.text: materialman’s liens and common interest association liens, are not eligible for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subordinate mortgage payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Closing Costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is permitted to deduct reasonable Closing Costs customarily paid by a seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the jurisdiction where the Mortgaged Premises are located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Real estate broker commissions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless a real estate broker’s sales commission exceeds 6% of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales price, the Servicer must not, as a condition of the Servicer’s acceptance of an offer,
elements.label: text

elements.text: renegotiate the real estate broker’s sales commission to an amount that is lower than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount that was originally agreed upon between the broker and the Borrower. In the
elements.label: text

elements.text: event the sales commission exceeds 6%, the Servicer must renegotiate the commission to
elements.label: text

elements.text: limit it to 6% of the Mortgaged Premises’ sales price.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Neither the Borrower nor the purchaser may receive a commission from the sale of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Short sale negotiation fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fees paid to any party to evaluate, negotiate or process a short sale with the Servicer,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which are commonly referred to as “short sale negotiation fees,” “short sale processing
elements.label: text

elements.text: fees,” “marketing fees,” or “administrative fees,” may be included as part of the real
elements.label: text

elements.text: estate broker’s commission at the discretion of the real estate broker. Negotiation fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: must not be deducted from sales proceeds or charged to the Borrower. Additionally,
elements.label: text

elements.text: neither the Servicer nor its agents may charge Freddie Mac or the Borrower, either
elements.label: text

elements.text: directly or indirectly, any fee whatsoever in connection with processing a short sale on
elements.label: text

elements.text: any Mortgage. Standard and customary real estate commissions and settlement service
elements.label: text

elements.text: fees agreed to by the Borrower and paid to the real estate brokerage and settlement agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: are not prohibited.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii)Waiver of Escrow funds or prepaid items
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must waive reimbursement of any Escrow, buydown funds or prepaid
elements.label: text

elements.text: items and assign any insurance proceeds to Freddie Mac, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (viii) Short sale affidavit
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain a short sale affidavit in which the parties to the transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: attest that the sale is an arm’s length transaction. An arm’s length transaction is a
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction between parties who are unrelated and unaffiliated by family, marriage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: commercial enterprise, other than the purchase and sale of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: between the Borrower(s) and the purchaser(s) that is the specific subject of the proposed
elements.label: text

elements.text: short sale as disclosed to the Servicer. This affidavit must be executed before or at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time of closing of the sale of the Mortgaged Premises by all Borrower(s), purchaser(s),
elements.label: text

elements.text: real estate brokers representing any of the parties, the escrow/closing agent and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction facilitator (if any). Each signatory must certify under penalty of perjury that to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the best of his or her knowledge and belief:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The sale of the Mortgaged Premises is an arm’s length transaction between parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: who are unrelated and unaffiliated by family, marriage or commercial enterprise
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There are no agreements, understandings or contracts between the parties that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower will remain in the Mortgaged Premises as a tenant or later obtain title or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership of the Mortgaged Premises, except if the Borrower is permitted to remain
elements.label: text

elements.text: as a tenant on the Mortgaged Premises for a short term, but no longer than ninety (90)
elements.label: text

elements.text: days, in order to facilitate relocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Neither the Borrower(s) nor the purchaser(s) will receive any funds or commissions
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the sale of the Mortgaged Premises. The Borrower may receive a relocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance payment if it is offered by the Servicer and reflected on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The seller(s)/listing agent has presented all offers for the purchase of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises to the Borrower and no offers have been held, concealed or delayed due to
elements.label: text

elements.text: action or inaction by a real estate agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There are no agreements, understandings or contracts relating to the current sale or
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsequent sale of the Mortgaged Premises that have not been disclosed to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All amounts to be paid to any party, including holders of other liens on the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises, in connection with the short sale transaction have been disclosed to and
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved by the Servicer and will be reflected on the Settlement/Closing Disclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each signatory understands, agrees and intends that the Servicer and Freddie Mac are
elements.label: text

elements.text: relying upon the statements made in the affidavit as consideration for the reduction of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the payoff amount of the Mortgage and agreement to the sale of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A signatory who makes a negligent or intentional misrepresentation agrees to
elements.label: text

elements.text: indemnify the Servicer and Freddie Mac for any and all loss resulting from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: misrepresentation, including, but not limited to, repayment of the amount of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reduced payoff of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The certification will survive the closing of the transaction; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each signatory understands that a misrepresentation may subject the party making the
elements.label: text

elements.text: misrepresentation to civil and/or criminal liability
elements.label: text

elements.text: The affidavit must contain the name of the Servicer, the Servicer loan number for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject Mortgage, the property address of the Mortgaged Premises and the date the sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract that is the subject of the short sale was ratified by the parties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The affidavit must contain the printed name and signature of each signatory, and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: signatures to the affidavit must be dated. The signature of a real estate agent or settlement
elements.label: text

elements.text: agent signing as a representative for the brokerage or settlement service provider is
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable so long as the representative capacity is clearly identified. The Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: modify and integrate its own requirements into the affidavit so long as it contains the
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum requirements contained in this section. The short sale affidavit must be a
elements.label: text

elements.text: separately identifiable document, distinct from other closing or pre-closing documents,
elements.label: text

elements.text: such as the sales contract. (See Exhibit 97, Short Sale Affidavit, for an example of a short
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale affidavit.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the closing agent is prohibited from signing the affidavit by applicable local, State or
elements.label: text

elements.text: federal law, the Servicer may waive the closing agent’s signature requirement upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: request. The Servicer must condition the waiver upon the closing agent’s agreement that
elements.label: text

elements.text: it will not also act as the closing agent on a subsequent transaction involving the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises within one year of closing the short sale transaction. In all other
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances, signatures from all parties identified above are required as a condition to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s agreement to accept a short sale of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a party reveals an agreement, understanding or contract relating to the current sale or
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsequent sale of the Mortgaged Premises that indicates the transaction is not an arm’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: length transaction or otherwise indicates bad faith, collusion or fraud on the part of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: parties, the Servicer must withdraw agreement to the short sale and immediately notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac using Freddie Mac’s Tip Referral Tool, accessible via Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Gateway.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ix)Re-sale deed restriction
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must instruct the settlement agent to ensure that the deed conveying the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises from the Borrower to the purchaser contains a provision to restrict
elements.label: text

elements.text: any re-sale of the Mortgaged Premises for 30 days following the short sale closing and to
elements.label: text

elements.text: restrict re-sales of greater than 120% of the short sale’s sales price for the period
elements.label: text

elements.text: beginning 31 days after the short sale closing and ending 90 days from the short sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: closing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The deed must contain the following provision:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Grantee herein is prohibited from conveying captioned property for any sales price for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: period of 30 days from [DATE – short sale closing]. After this 30-day period, Grantee is
elements.label: text

elements.text: further prohibited from conveying the property for a sales price greater than $ (120% of
elements.label: text

elements.text: short sale price) until 90 days from [DATE – short sale closing]. These restrictions shall
elements.label: text

elements.text: run with the land and are not personal to the Grantee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The provided language may be amended as necessary to comply with applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Servicer believes a re-sale restriction is not appropriate for a particular short
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale transaction (such as a short sale transaction under the Homeowners Assistance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Program provided by the U.S. Department of Defense), then the Servicer must submit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage to Freddie Mac for review prior to instructing the settlement agent to insert the
elements.label: text

elements.text: above referenced deed provision. The Servicer’s submission must include a thorough
elements.label: text

elements.text: explanation of the reason(s) why it is requesting additional review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (x) Resolve
elements.label: text

elements.text: Services must use Resolve to submit short sale transactions on Freddie Mac-owned
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages. Each Servicer must use Resolve in accordance with (A) the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: this Chapter, (B) the short sale and other instructions provided in Resolve Online Help
elements.label: text

elements.text: and any other Documentation and (C) any other applicable provisions of the Guide,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including Sections 2401.1 and 2404.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer is unable to complete a submission via Resolve, the Servicer should contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xi)Mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must approve and process a short sale in compliance with all requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of applicable mortgage insurance policies so as to preserve and not to impair existing
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance coverage, if any. When approving and processing a short sale, unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac has delegations of authority with the MI, the Servicer must either obtain the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable MI’s approval of the terms of each short sale on a case-by-case basis or ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the applicable MI has provided a delegation of authority to the Servicer that applies
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the requested short sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is notified that the MI will curtail or deny a claim for any reason,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to, failure of the Servicer to comply with mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions such as payment of a Borrower contribution, the Servicer may not approve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: short sale; however, the Servicer must submit the proposed short sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9208.3: Closing, reporting and compliance for short
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Closing, reporting, drafting and remittance requirements for short sales and make-whole
elements.label: text

elements.text: preforeclosure sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fraudulent transactions related to short sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Closing, reporting, drafting and remittance requirements for short sales and make￾whole preforeclosure sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Servicer review of the Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale (“short sale”) closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Prior to and outside of the transaction, pay any delinquent property taxes, ground
elements.label: text

elements.text: rents and assessments or other charges that are or may become First Liens on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property or that if not paid would result in the subordination of Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: interests. (See Section 9301.6(e) regarding expenses that may become First Liens on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property.) Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for these expenses in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Chapter 9701.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Ensure that the Borrower pays all cash contributions in the form of cash or certified
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds at settlement
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Ensure that the title is conveyed directly from the Borrower to the buyer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Complete the short sale closing within 60 days of approving the purchase offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Waive its rights to any accrued late fees or property inspection costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Maintain the original short sale affidavit in the Mortgage file in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Section 3302.1(c) and provide Freddie Mac with a copy of the short
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale affidavit upon request
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Review the Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement prior to closing to ensure proper
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer of title directly from the Borrower to the buyer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Review the Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement and deed within five Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days after closing to validate compliance with this chapter and the Servicer’s approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The name of the buyer on the Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement is the
elements.label: text

elements.text: same as shown on the sales contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement is consistent with the closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions, especially regarding ineligible transfer of title to related parties; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The deed will be recorded in the name of the buyer and contains resale restriction
elements.label: text

elements.text: language as required in Section 9208.2(c)(ix)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. For Cooperative Share Loans, comply with the requirements in Section 8801.3(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Reporting the transaction, remitting the proceeds and submitting the settlement
elements.label: text

elements.text: data
elements.label: text

elements.text: To close a short sale or a make-whole preforeclosure sale in Freddie Mac’s systems,
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete the following accounting, reporting and remittance steps:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Report the Mortgage as a “Short Sale/Charge-off/Make-whole” via the Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Level Reporting tool (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) by the second Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Day after the Servicer receives the settlement proceeds. When reporting, ensure that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ending UPB is zero
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Principal collected is the ending balance of the Mortgage (not the proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: collected from the sale of the property)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff date is the date the Servicer received the settlement proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ DDLPI reflects the due date of the last fully paid installment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will draft the full UPB plus exception interest. Refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8303.3(c) for details on payoff requirements and charge-off adjustments. See Sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8303.3(c) and 8303.3(d) for additional requirements related to short sale reporting and
elements.label: text

elements.text: drafting.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Complete the “Short Sales Settlement” screen in Resolve® and transmit the data to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac. In circumstances where a short sale settlement requires a manual
elements.label: text

elements.text: settlement review by Freddie Mac, Freddie Mac may request copies of documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to, the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Copies of the sales contract and any addenda to the sales contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Make-whole preforeclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: To notify Freddie Mac that the Servicer has approved a make-whole preforeclosure sale,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must submit the following to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completed and signed Form 710, Mortgage Assistance Application (or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation as permitted in Section 9208.1(c))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Copy of the executed sales contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Copy of the MI’s approval letter (if applicable and if not previously delegated by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: MI)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A breakdown of the transaction to show how the sale of the Mortgaged Premises plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: any other proceeds will result in a total satisfaction of the debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon receipt of the required documentation as described above, Freddie Mac will review
elements.label: text

elements.text: the file and contact the Servicer with a decision in Resolve based on the Servicer’s input.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the Servicer receives the decision, it must process the file accordingly and report
elements.label: text

elements.text: and close the transaction in accordance with the requirements in this Section 9208.3(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will draft the full UPB plus exception interest. Refer to Section 8303.3(d)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for details on payoff requirements and charge-off adjustments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Charging off the deficiency
elements.label: text

elements.text: The amount that Freddie Mac has determined to be charged off will be reflected on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Draft Report. The Servicer must review the Draft Report and report any discrepancies
elements.label: text

elements.text: between its records and the amount on the Draft Report to Freddie Mac via the Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Servicing Data Corrections tool (see Exhibit 88) within the same Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: in which Freddie Mac posts the amount to the Draft Report. Servicers may access the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Draft Report through the Cash Manager tool (see Exhibit 88).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When reporting a discrepancy, Servicers must input the calculation used to determine the
elements.label: text

elements.text: variance and upload a copy of the Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement and of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales contract and any addenda to the sales contract in the Servicing Data Corrections tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: to support the request and explain why the short sale and any related deficiency was not
elements.label: text

elements.text: initially settled with data elements matching these documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Discrepancies submitted after the Accounting Cycle in which the initial adjustment is
elements.label: text

elements.text: posted to the Draft Report closes will be processed at Freddie Mac’s discretion and may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be subject to a contract noncompliance and contract change compensatory fee (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8303.5(i)). If the postsettlement correction request is denied, the Servicer may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: liable for any additional losses. Additionally, Freddie Mac may recover any workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: incentives that were paid.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Remitting additional proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there are any proceeds that were not included in the proceeds check (e.g., property
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance premium rebate, refunded Escrow advance prepayments), remit the full amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: of such proceeds to Freddie Mac by wire transfer or check and send it to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Directory 5) within five Business Days of receipt of the proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Release of deficiency if participants have acted in good faith and in compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: all applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has acted in good faith and in compliance with the Guide and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable local, State and federal law, then Freddie Mac will not pursue the Borrower for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the entire amount owed under the current Mortgage. Freddie Mac will accept the
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds of sale and the cash contribution by the Borrower and the Servicer must instruct
elements.label: text

elements.text: the closing agent to release the lien on the Mortgaged Premises and mark the previous
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note as canceled. For a short sale completed with respect to an eMortgage (as defined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 1402.1(b)), the Servicer must also comply with the requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, if the Borrower had knowledge of and/or consented to a transaction that was
elements.label: text

elements.text: not in compliance with the Guide and all applicable local, State and federal law, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac reserves its rights to pursue any and all participants including, but not limited to, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, for the full amount owed under the Mortgage, the amount of any other loss or
elements.label: text

elements.text: damage Freddie Mac may have suffered and other costs and expenses, including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, attorney fees. Failure to fully execute and provide the short sale affidavit
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by Section 9208.2(c) is considered bad faith and noncompliance with the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii) Requesting reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Request reimbursement for any applicable expenses in accordance with the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Chapter 9701. For claim submissions on Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the VA or RHS not subject to recourse or indemnification, expense reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: will occur after Freddie Mac receives the claim payment from the applicable entity and
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines that all other requirements for reimbursement have been satisfied. (Refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9603.2(a) on FHA/VA/RHS claim filing and Section 9603.2(d) on receipt of
elements.label: text

elements.text: claim payment.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Fraudulent transactions related to short sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any party to the transaction who engages in fraudulent activity with respect to a short sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction may be added to the Freddie Mac Exclusionary List, barred from future
elements.label: text

elements.text: involvement in Freddie Mac business and reported to applicable regulatory authorities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Chapter 3201 for more information on Freddie Mac requirements for fraud prevention,
elements.label: text

elements.text: detection and reporting.

elements.text: Chapter 9209: Freddie Mac Standard Deed-in￾Lieu of Foreclosure
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 9209.1: Deed-in-lieu of foreclosure eligibility
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements and Servicer approval authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Freddie Mac Standard Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure (“deed-in-lieu of foreclosure”) is a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s voluntary conveyance of clear and marketable title to the property to Freddie Mac in
elements.label: text

elements.text: exchange for a discharge of debt.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligibility requirements for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer approval authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submitting a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure recommendation to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must use the Resolve® user interface (UI) to submit a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommendation to Freddie Mac. Servicers that elect to submit deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommendations via the Resolve UI should refer to Resolve Online Help for details on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s eligible hardship is permanent or long term and neither a home retention
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure nor a Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale (“short sale”) is a viable
elements.label: text

elements.text: solution to the Delinquency or imminent Delinquency, then the Servicer must determine if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower meets the eligibility requirements for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluate the Borrower for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure under this chapter once the Servicer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise complied with the evaluation hierarchy in Section 9201.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is subject to a recourse agreement, the Mortgage is ineligible for a deed-in-lieu
elements.label: text

elements.text: of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, Mortgages for which the Borrower is engaged in litigation related to the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Mortgaged Premises, with the exception of a foreclosure action, are ineligible for a deed-in￾lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is an FHA, VA or Guaranteed Rural Housing Mortgage, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with the requirements of the applicable agency when approving a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure in a manner that ensures continued coverage of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must determine the Borrower’s Delinquency status for purposes of establishing
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility, documentation requirements and Borrower contribution requirements in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Sections 9209.1 through 9209.3. The Servicer must use the Delinquency status of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage on the date the Servicer begins the evaluation of the Borrower for a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure using the documentation required in Section 9209.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligibility requirements for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Every Borrower, regardless of Delinquency status, is eligible to be considered for a deed-in￾lieu of foreclosure, provided the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers who do not meet the requirements for a Streamlined Deed-in-Lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure must be experiencing or have experienced one of the eligible hardships listed
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 9202.1(b)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must be able to convey clear and marketable title to the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower has not acquired a new Mortgage in the six months preceding the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Delinquency or, if the Borrower is current, in the six months preceding the
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation of the Borrower for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure. The Borrower is only
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted to have obtained a new Mortgage if the Borrower’s eligible hardship was
elements.label: text

elements.text: distant employment transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the debt secured by the Mortgaged Premises has been discharged in a bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeding filed pursuant to Chapter 7 of the U.S. Bankruptcy Code, the Borrower is eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure regardless of Delinquency, occupancy or property type. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower must provide the Servicer a copy of the order and accompanying documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: showing that the debt was discharged. The Servicer must retain those documents in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower who is current or less than 60 days delinquent must meet the imminent default
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements as described in Section 9209.2(b)(iv).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Servicer approval authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise notified by Freddie Mac, all Servicers are delegated to approve a deed-in￾lieu of foreclosure that meets the eligibility requirements of Section 9209.1(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When evaluating a Borrower who is a service member with Permanent Change of Station
elements.label: text

elements.text: orders and is current or less than 90 days delinquent, the Servicer is encouraged to submit a
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommendation to Freddie Mac for review if the Servicer believes a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure is the most appropriate option given the Borrower’s circumstances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a property inspection reveals that the property has been poorly maintained, needs major
elements.label: text

elements.text: repairs or has structural or foundation problems, then the Servicer is not delegated to approve
elements.label: text

elements.text: a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure. However, if the Servicer believes a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: is still the most appropriate remedy, then the Servicer must submit a recommendation to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac for approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must refer to the requirements in Sections 8801.3(d) and 9209.1 through 9209.6 for
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional circumstances where Servicers are not delegated to approve a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Property requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the BPO obtained according to the valuation requirements in Section 9209.5(d) or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: previous or subsequent Servicer inspection indicates that the Mortgaged Premises has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: poorly maintained, needs major repairs or has structural or foundation problems, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: is not delegated to approve a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure. However, if the Servicer believes a
elements.label: text

elements.text: deed-in-lieu of foreclosure is still the most appropriate remedy, then the Servicer must submit
elements.label: text

elements.text: a recommendation to Freddie Mac for approval in accordance with Section 9209.1(b).
elements.label: text
