elements.text: In addition, if the Servicer has any indication that the Mortgaged Premises contains
elements.label: text

elements.text: environmental contamination or hazardous substances, the Servicer is not delegated to
elements.label: text

elements.text: approve a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure. Examples of environmental contamination and
elements.label: text

elements.text: hazardous substances include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ High sulfur building content, such as drywall
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interior mold
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exposed asbestos
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exposed or chipping lead-based paint
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evidence of illegal activity having taken place on the premises, such as growing or
elements.label: text

elements.text: manufacturing illegal substances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgaged Premises is or is part of a Superfund site
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgaged Premises exhibits other conditions that could negatively impact the health of
elements.label: text

elements.text: occupants
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Submitting a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure recommendation to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: For situations where a Borrower does not meet the eligibility requirements for a deed-in-lieu
elements.label: text

elements.text: of foreclosure and the Servicer feels a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure may be the best option for
elements.label: text

elements.text: addressing the Delinquency or imminent Delinquency, the Servicer may submit an exception
elements.label: text

elements.text: review request through Resolve. For Cooperative Share Loans, the additional documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: listed in Section 8801.3(d) must be included with the Servicer’s recommendation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9209.2: Borrower documentation for deeds-in-lieu of
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: foreclosure (10/08/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluating the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Borrower documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Borrower documentation requirements for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower documentation requirements for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure by Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: status
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency status at of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the time of evaluation is…
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must…
elements.label: text

elements.text: Current or less than 90 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evaluate the Borrower based on a complete Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response Package as defined in Section 9102.5.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Mortgage is current or less than 60 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent, the Servicer must determine that the Borrower's
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payment is in non-retention imminent default in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 9209.2(b)(iv).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Between 90 days and 18
elements.label: text

elements.text: months delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evaluate the Borrower based on a complete Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response Package, unless one of the following conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: applies:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower failed a Freddie Mac Flex Modification®
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trial Period Plan within the 12 months prior to evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a short sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower previously received a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification and become 60 days or more delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: within the first 12 months of the effective date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification without curing the Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower previously completed three or more
elements.label: text

elements.text: modifications; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower received a forbearance plan as a result of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: hardship due to their Mortgaged Premises or places of
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment being located in an Eligible Disaster Area or
elements.label: text

elements.text: COVID-19 and became 90 days or more delinquent prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the evaluation for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is not secured by an Investment Property,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as identified at origination, and the Borrower’s FICO®
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Score is less than or equal to 620
elements.label: text

elements.text: In these cases, the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Streamlined Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure (“Streamlined
elements.label: text

elements.text: DIL”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Greater than 18 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evaluate the Borrower for a Streamlined DIL.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Streamlined DIL is a Standard Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: where the Servicer is not required to obtain the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response Package or to verify an eligible hardship.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Credit Score requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower’s Credit Score must be no more than 90 days old as of the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer evaluates the Borrower for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure. If there is more than
elements.label: text

elements.text: one Borrower on the Mortgage, the Servicer must choose one Credit Score that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: adequately indicative of the credit reputation of all Borrowers currently on the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must first select a single Credit Score for each Borrower on the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer obtains multiple Credit Scores for a single Borrower, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: use the middle/lower method to select the single Credit Score for that Borrower. If there
elements.label: text

elements.text: are multiple Borrowers on the Mortgage, the Servicer must determine the single Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Score for each Borrower using the method described above. The Servicer must then
elements.label: text

elements.text: select either the lowest Credit Score across all Borrowers on the Mortgage or the average
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Score from all Borrowers’ single scores. Whichever method is used, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must choose the single Credit Score using the same method and procedure for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers and for all Mortgages consistent with fair lending laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If during the current period of Delinquency, the Borrower was determined eligible for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Streamlined Short Sale (refer to Section 9208.1(c)), then the Borrower is also eligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Streamlined DIL outlined above, even if the Credit Score that was used previously to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine eligibility for the Streamlined Short Sale is now more than 90 days old at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time of the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure evaluation. However, if the Borrower has since
elements.label: text

elements.text: brought the Mortgage current, then the Borrower is no longer eligible for a Streamlined
elements.label: text

elements.text: Short Sale or Streamlined DIL and is required to submit a complete Borrower Response
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package to the Servicer to be evaluated for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Evaluating the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Evaluating the credit report
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain a credit report for each Borrower on the Mortgage (or a joint
elements.label: text

elements.text: report for co-Borrowers). The credit report must be no more than 90 days old as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: date the Servicer evaluates the Borrower for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must review the credit report to verify that the Borrower meets the relevant requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure and to evaluate the Borrower’s ability to make a
elements.label: text

elements.text: contribution.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Verifying occupancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Borrowers who are current or less than 60 days delinquent, the Servicer must review
elements.label: text

elements.text: the credit report to verify that at least one of the Borrowers is occupying the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises as a Primary Residence. If the credit report does not indicate that the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is the Primary Residence for at least one Borrower, then the Servicer must use
elements.label: text

elements.text: good business judgment to reconcile the inconsistency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)New Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless the Borrower is eligible for a Streamlined DIL, the Servicer must review the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s credit report to determine whether the Borrower obtained a new Mortgage(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the six months preceding the Borrower’s Delinquency or, if the Borrower is current, in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the six months preceding the evaluation of the Borrower for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer’s review of the credit report reveals a new Mortgage, the Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: approve the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure only if the eligible hardship for a Borrower who
elements.label: text

elements.text: was 90 or more days delinquent was due to distant employment transfer, relocation due to
elements.label: text

elements.text: new employment or Permanent Change of Station (PCS) orders and the Servicer verifies
elements.label: text

elements.text: that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower intends to occupy the property securing the new Mortgage as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The new employment location is greater than 50 miles one way from the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The new property address is reasonably near the Borrower’s new employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: location
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has any other hardship, or if the above referenced hardship was for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower who was less than 90 days delinquent, and the Servicer determines a new
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage has been obtained, the Servicer is not delegated to approve the deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure and must submit the request to Freddie Mac for consideration.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer’s review of the credit report indicates that a mortgage creditor has made an
elements.label: text

elements.text: inquiry within the previous four-month period, the Servicer must contact the Borrower to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine the following on the Mortgage sought:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The address of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The purpose of the inquiry (e.g., refinance or purchase Mortgage) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The result of the inquiry (e.g., refinance or purchase Mortgage is pending, closed or
elements.label: text

elements.text: canceled)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a purchase Mortgage was obtained, then the Servicer is not delegated to approve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, and the file must be sent Freddie Mac. Refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9209.1 regarding the submission of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Special requirements for Borrowers who are current or less than 60 days delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower is current or less than 60 days delinquent, he or she must be considered to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be in non-retention imminent default following the business rules in the chart below
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless the Borrower was discharged from a Chapter 7 bankruptcy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Non-retention imminent default evaluation business rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resolve® will evaluate the information the Servicer provides against the imminent
elements.label: text

elements.text: default business rules. Any Borrower who is current or less than 60 days delinquent at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the time the Servicer commences the initial evaluation is in non-retention imminent
elements.label: text

elements.text: default if the Borrower meets the requirements of the following business rules:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Non-retention imminent default evaluation business rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be considered in non-retention imminent default, the Borrower must meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: all requirements under business rule 1 and must meet the requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 2, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 3
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business rule 1 Each Borrower must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submit a complete Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: Non-retention imminent default evaluation business rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be considered in non-retention imminent default, the Borrower must meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: all requirements under business rule 1 and must meet the requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 2, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 3
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be current or less than 60 days delinquent (i.e., less than
elements.label: text

elements.text: three monthly payments past due) on the Mortgage as of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the evaluation date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Occupy the property as a Primary Residence (or at least
elements.label: text

elements.text: one Borrower on the Mortgage must occupy the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property as his or her Primary Residence)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have Cash Reserves (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have an eligible hardship as described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Requirements related to occupancy and non￾retirement liquid assets do not apply if the Borrower is a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicemember (as defined in Section 8503.1) with PCS
elements.label: text

elements.text: orders and the property securing the Mortgage is or was the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Primary Residence where the transfer or new
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment location is greater than 50 miles one way from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property securing the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business rule 2 The Borrower is considered in non-retention imminent
elements.label: text

elements.text: default if the Borrower meets the requirements of business
elements.label: text

elements.text: rule 1, and:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s Credit Score is less than or equal to 620
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined in accordance with Section 9206.1(e)(v);
elements.label: text

elements.text: AND
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage has had two or more 30-day
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquencies in the most recent 6-month period; OR
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s housing expense-to-income ratio is
elements.label: text

elements.text: greater than 40% as of the evaluation date
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has one of the Imminent Default Hardships
elements.label: text

elements.text: described below in business rule 3, the Borrower may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined to be in non-retention imminent default even if
elements.label: text

elements.text: these business rule 2 requirements are not met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must submit all information for business rule 1 and business rule 2
elements.label: text

elements.text: in all instances, even if the Borrower does not meet the requirements under
elements.label: text

elements.text: business rule 2 and instead is approved based on the Imminent Default Hardship
elements.label: text

elements.text: under business rule 3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B)Income and asset documentation and verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: (I) Documentation and verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be evaluated for imminent default, a Borrower must, at a minimum, provide a
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete Borrower Response Package as defined in Section 9102.5(c). In addition
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the income documentation required under Section 9202.1(c), the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain the Borrower’s Credit Score in accordance with Section 9206.1(e)(v).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Non-retention imminent default evaluation business rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be considered in non-retention imminent default, the Borrower must meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: all requirements under business rule 1 and must meet the requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 2, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 3
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business rule 3 The Borrower is considered in non-retention imminent
elements.label: text

elements.text: default if the Borrower meets the requirements of business
elements.label: text

elements.text: rule 1 and the Borrower provided the documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: required in Section 9202.1(b) supporting one of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Imminent Default Hardships listed below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Death of a Borrower or death of either the primary or
elements.label: text

elements.text: secondary wage earner in the household
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Long-term or permanent disability or serious illness of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower/co-Borrower or dependent family member
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Divorce or legal separation or separation of Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: unrelated by marriage, civil union or similar domestic
elements.label: text

elements.text: partnership under applicable law; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Distant employment transfer or relocation due to new
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment or PCS orders where the property securing
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage being evaluated is the Borrower’s Primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residence. The new employment location must be more
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 50 miles one way from the property securing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage being evaluated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Imminent Default Hardship must currently cause and
elements.label: text

elements.text: be expected to continue to cause a long-term or permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: decrease in income or increase in expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (II)Verification of income and assets; resolution of material inconsistencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must review all documentation submitted by the Borrower to identify
elements.label: text

elements.text: any material inconsistencies, including material inconsistencies with a tax return
elements.label: text

elements.text: or tax transcript if one was obtained under Section 9202.1(c). If, based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s good business judgment, there are material inconsistencies with respect
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the income or asset information disclosed by the Borrower or with other
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation relevant to the imminent default decision, the Servicer must obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: other documentation to reasonably reconcile such material inconsistencies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must also document such material differences in their Servicing system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer cannot reconcile such material differences, the Borrower cannot be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered in imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C)Cash Reserves test
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete an evaluation of the Borrower’s Cash Reserves. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower must have Cash Reserves of less than $25,000 to be further evaluated for
elements.label: text

elements.text: imminent default. If the Borrower either discloses or provides documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicating the Borrower has Cash Reserves equal to or greater than $25,000, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is not in imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (I) Definition of Cash Reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of determining imminent default, Cash Reserves are defined as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash Reserves: Any non-retirement liquid asset the Borrower has available for
elements.label: text

elements.text: withdrawal from any financial institution or brokerage, including funds on deposit
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Borrower’s checking, savings, money market or certificate of deposit
elements.label: text

elements.text: account or other depository account, stocks, bonds, mutual funds, U.S.
elements.label: text

elements.text: government securities and other securities that are traded on an exchange or
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketplace generally available to the public (e.g., New York Stock Exchange,
elements.label: text

elements.text: National Association of Securities Dealers Automated Quotations, Midwest SE,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chicago Board of Trade or Over the Counter) for which the price can be readily
elements.label: text

elements.text: verified through financial publications.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assets are only considered retirement assets if they are held in a qualified
elements.label: text

elements.text: retirement account such as a 401k, 403b, 457, Individual Retirement Account or
elements.label: text

elements.text: pension fund. If the assets are not held in a retirement account, the assets must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered Cash Reserves.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (II)Calculating Cash Reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must calculate the Borrower’s Cash Reserves in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must determine that, for every Borrower on the Mortgage, all the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Cash Reserves have been accounted for on Form 710, Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assistance Application
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In making the determination that all Cash Reserves have been accounted for,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must review all information provided by the Borrower to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine if the asset information stated on Form 710 is reasonably consistent
elements.label: text

elements.text: with information available from all other information provided by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, including verbal information shared by the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If there are inconsistencies between the Borrower’s disclosure of assets and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the information provided by the Borrower, then the Servicer must obtain the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s tax return or tax transcript to reconcile the inconsistencies. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If, upon reviewing the Borrower’s tax return or tax transcript, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, the Servicer observes interest, dividend income or gains/losses
elements.label: text

elements.text: that, in total, that could not be reasonably produced by the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclosed Cash Reserves, and such income indicates deposits, securities
elements.label: text

elements.text: holdings or other assets that could be in excess of the amounts disclosed
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Borrower on Form 710, the Servicer must reconcile the
elements.label: text

elements.text: inconsistency with the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer must require the Borrower to produce a signed federal tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: return and all relevant schedules, in the event the Servicer used a tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: transcript in lieu of a tax return, along with any other relevant
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation that verifies the disposition and/or current status of those
elements.label: text

elements.text: assets, which produced the income or gains/losses to resolve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: inconsistency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer must ensure that the Borrower’s disclosure of assets is
elements.label: text

elements.text: reasonably accurate despite the inconsistency between the disclosed assets
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the income or gain/loss from assets reported on the tax return or tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: transcript
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ In determining what documentation is needed to reconcile an
elements.label: text

elements.text: inconsistency, the Servicer must review the detailed tax return schedules
elements.label: text

elements.text: and forms and request from the Borrower copies of recent and past
elements.label: text

elements.text: statements from those asset holdings or transactions indicated on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: schedules and forms that produced the income or gain/loss (e.g., checking,
elements.label: text

elements.text: savings, brokerage account statements, asset sale statements or records)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If there are inconsistencies between the Borrower’s disclosure of assets and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the tax return information that cannot be reconciled, the Borrower cannot be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered in imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that the Borrower has Cash Reserves of less than
elements.label: text

elements.text: $25,000 and meets all other requirements of Section 9206.1(e)(ii), then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is considered to be in imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (D)Imminent default Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must choose one Credit Score that is adequately indicative of the credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: reputation of all Borrowers currently on the Mortgage. Servicers must use a Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Score based on the credit-scoring model. This score must be obtained and determined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with the requirements below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (I) Obtaining Credit Scores for each Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must request a Credit Score for each Borrower on the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: from any one of the following three credit repositories:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Equifax Credit Information Services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Experian Information Systems and Services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ TransUnion Credit Information Company
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower’s Credit Score must be less than 90 days old on the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer performs the imminent default evaluation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (II)Borrowers with no available Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: It is unusual for any Borrower who has obtained a Mortgage not to have a Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Score. If no single Credit Score can be identified for a Borrower, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must recheck the information provided when ordering the Credit Scores and
elements.label: text

elements.text: resubmit a request. If the Servicer is still unable to obtain a Credit Score for that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, it may rely on the Credit Scores of all other Borrowers as determined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Absent a Credit Score for any Borrower on the Mortgage, the Borrower may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: be determined to be in imminent default under the requirements of business rule
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2, and the Servicer must proceed to evaluate the Borrower under the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of business rule 3 in Section 9206.1(e)(ii). When a Credit Score is not available
elements.label: text

elements.text: for any Borrower on the Mortgage, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain documentation in the Mortgage file that demonstrates the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: attempts to obtain Credit Scores from all three credit repositories on all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Enter the result that a FICO score is not available for any Borrower on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage into Resolve
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Proceed to the Imminent Default Hardship test in business rule 3 to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: if an Imminent Default Hardship exists
elements.label: text

elements.text: (III)Determining the Imminent Default Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must identify the Imminent Default Credit Score in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must first select a single Credit Score for each Borrower on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage. If the Servicer obtains multiple Credit Scores for a single
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, the Servicer must use the middle/lower method to select the single
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Score for that Borrower. This method is the most predictive when
elements.label: text

elements.text: determining a single Borrower's overall credit reputation. If three Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Scores are obtained for a Borrower, the single score for that Borrower is the
elements.label: text

elements.text: one with the middle value. For example, if the Credit Scores were 660, 656
elements.label: text

elements.text: and 640, the single Credit Score selected by the Servicer should be 656. When
elements.label: text

elements.text: there is a duplicate score, the Seller must select that score to be the single
elements.label: text

elements.text: score. If the Credit Scores for a Borrower were 660, 660 and 640, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: should select 660. If two Credit Scores were obtained for a Borrower, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must select the lower of the two Credit Scores to be the single Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Score for that Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If there is only one Borrower on the Mortgage, the single Credit Score,
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined in accordance with the above requirements, is considered the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Imminent Default Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If there are multiple Borrowers on the Mortgage, the Servicer must determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: the single Credit Score for each Borrower using the method described above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must then select either the lowest Credit Score across all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers on the Mortgage or the average Credit Score from all Borrowers'
elements.label: text

elements.text: single scores. (Note: Whichever method is used, the Servicer should choose
elements.label: text

elements.text: the single Credit Score using the same method and procedure for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers and for all Mortgages consistent with fair lending laws.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (E) Calculating housing expense-to-income ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must input verified income data into Resolve. Based on the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: input, Resolve will determine if the Borrower’s housing expense-to-income ratio is
elements.label: text

elements.text: greater than 40%. For purposes of this determination, the Borrower’s current monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing expense is divided by the Borrower’s monthly gross income (or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers’ combined monthly gross income in the case of co-Borrowers) plus any
elements.label: text

elements.text: allowable non-obligor household income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower’s current monthly housing expense consists of the following, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly principal and interest payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly pro rata amount for real estate taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly pro rata amount for property or flood insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly pro rata amount for homeowners association (HOA) dues, Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unit or Cooperative Unit Maintenance Fees and ground rent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any Escrow shortage currently included as part of the monthly contractual
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has been granted interest rate relief under the Servicemembers Civil
elements.label: text

elements.text: Relief Act (SCRA), the Servicer must use the principal and interest payment and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: contractual rate of interest in effect on the Note prior to the granting of the SCRA
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief rather than the temporarily SCRA-reduced interest rate and related SCRA
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payment when calculating the Borrower’s current monthly housing expense￾to-income ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower has indicated that there are condominium/HOA or Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation assessments (see Chapter 8801 for special Servicing requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans), Condominium Unit maintenance fees or Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Maintenance Fees or ground rents but has not been able to provide written
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation to verify these amounts, the Servicer must rely on the information
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided by the Borrower if the Servicer has made reasonable efforts to obtain the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts in writing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The current monthly housing expense does not include mortgage insurance premium
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments or payments due to holders of subordinate liens.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on the information provided by the Servicer, Resolve will calculate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s housing-to-expense income ratio and determine if it meets the imminent
elements.label: text

elements.text: default requirements under business rule 2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (F) Payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on the information provided by the Servicer, Resolve will review the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s payment history and determine if it meets the imminent default
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements under business rule 2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (G)Imminent default evaluation results
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower meets the requirements of business rule 1 and meets the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of either business rule 2 or business rule 3, the Borrower is in imminent default. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, and no further
elements.label: text

elements.text: analysis is required by the Servicer to determine imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (H)General requirements and information
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that a Borrower is in imminent default, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue evaluating the Borrower using the applicable requirements outlined in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapter to determine if the Borrower qualifies for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9209.3: Deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure Borrower
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: contributions and relocation assistance (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower contributions towards the deficiency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower relocation assistance
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Borrower contributions towards the deficiency
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that the Borrower’s Cash Reserves (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9209.2(b)(iv)(C)(I)) exceed $10,000 or the Borrower’s housing expense-to-income (HTI)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratio is less than or equal to 40%, the Servicer must request a cash contribution in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the requirements below. The Servicer must verbally confirm the assets reported on Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 710, Mortgage Assistance Application, and reconcile any differences with documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: following the procedure in Section 9202.1(c). A Servicer may negotiate contribution amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: less than the initial contribution requests, which must be determined in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: contribution formula in this section. When a Servicer negotiates a cash contribution that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: less than the initial request, the Servicer must document the reason for its decision in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file and note the specific financial circumstances that limit the Borrower’s ability
elements.label: text

elements.text: to contribute towards the deficiency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless Freddie Mac has delegated authority with the MI or communicates otherwise, if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is covered by mortgage insurance and the MI requires a contribution from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower that is greater than the contribution limits required by this section in order to
elements.label: text

elements.text: approve the Freddie Mac Standard Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure (“deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure”), the Servicer must require the Borrower to make the contribution required by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the MI as a condition of approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers are not required to make a contribution in the following instances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers who are Servicemembers (see Section 8503.1) with Permanent Change of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Station (PCS) orders who are 90 or more days delinquent. The property securing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is or was previously the Borrower’s Primary Residence where the transfer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: new employment location is greater than 50 miles one-way from the property securing
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage being evaluated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers who qualify for a Streamlined Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure (refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Applicable law prohibits requesting or receiving a contribution
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s Cash Reserves exceed $10,000 or his or her HTI ratio is less than or
elements.label: text

elements.text: equal to 40%, the Servicer must request the greater of 20% of the Cash Reserves or four
elements.label: text

elements.text: times the Borrower’s principal, interest, taxes and insurance, rounded to the nearest $100
elements.label: text

elements.text: as a cash contribution. The cash contribution cannot exceed the total amount of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: deficiency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower is 60 or more days delinquent and cannot contribute 20% of his or her Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reserves, the Servicer may negotiate a lower level of contribution. If the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: negotiates and collects less than 20% of the Borrower’s Cash Reserves, then the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must document in the Mortgage file the specific financial circumstances that limit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s ability to make the initially requested level of cash contribution (i.e., 20% of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s Cash Reserves).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on the Servicer’s assessment of the Borrower’s written or stated ability to pay in
elements.label: text

elements.text: combination with its evaluation of the Borrower’s financial and hardship information, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is authorized to negotiate a lower contribution or agree that the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual situation and circumstances warrant a lower starting point to cash contribution
elements.label: text

elements.text: negotiations or no contribution. If a Borrower is unwilling to contribute an amount the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer deems acceptable, then the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure is not delegated and must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be submitted to Freddie Mac in the manner outlined in Section 9209.1. If the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines the Borrower is unable to contribute at least $500 toward the deficiency, then
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must not collect a cash contribution.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s Cash Reserves are in excess of $50,000, the Servicer must submit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: deed-in-lieu of foreclosure request to Freddie Mac for review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9209.1 regarding the details for submitting a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure recommendation to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Borrower relocation assistance
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that the Borrower is not required to make a financial contribution
elements.label: text

elements.text: toward the deficiency, the Borrower is eligible to receive up to $7,500 in relocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance provided that the Borrower occupies the Mortgaged Premises as his or her primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: residence. The Borrower is not eligible to receive relocation assistance in the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower will receive relocation assistance from a source other than Freddie Mac or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer, such as an employer, and the amount is equal to or greater than $7,500. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower will receive relocation assistance from a source other than Freddie Mac or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer and the amount is less than $7,500, the Servicer must reduce the amount of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s relocation assistance by the amount received from the other source.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer determines that the Borrower’s Cash Reserves exceed the greater of
elements.label: text

elements.text: $10,000 or four times the Borrower’s total monthly Mortgage payment, regardless of
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the Borrower makes a contribution. If the Servicer believes such a Borrower is
elements.label: text

elements.text: experiencing financial circumstances necessitating relocation assistance, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may submit the file to Freddie Mac. Refer to Section 9209.1 regarding the details for
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower is subject to PCS orders and receives government assistance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: relocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the final amount of relocation assistance has been determined and the deed has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed, the Servicer must disburse to the Borrower the relocation assistance payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise instructed by Freddie Mac, the Servicers must distribute the relocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance funds no more than 30 days after execution of the deed. Servicers may request
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement for the relocation assistance from Freddie Mac via PAID (Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools).
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the relocation assistance offered by Freddie Mac, the Servicer may in its
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion offer the Borrower an additional financial incentive to complete the deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure. Any relocation assistance provided by the Servicer will be in addition to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: relocation assistance amount provided by Freddie Mac, and the Servicer’s assistance amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not have to be subtracted from the assistance amount Freddie Mac will pay. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer does offer its own relocation assistance, it must provide the assistance from its own
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds as a payment made directly to the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The relocation assistance payment may not be applied to other debts secured by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9209.4: Borrower communication and execution
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: timelines for deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the response requirements related to Borrower Response Packages addressed in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9102.5, the following chart sets forth the required response times when a Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standard Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure (“deed-in-lieu of foreclosure”) is being considered as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: solution to the Delinquency for either a Mortgage that is not secured by a Primary Residence or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage that is secured by Primary Residence and the Servicer is not evaluating the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure using the First Complete Borrower Response Package received
elements.label: text

elements.text: more than 37 days prior to a scheduled foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deed-in-lieu of foreclosure: submission of Borrower Response Package or
elements.label: text

elements.text: streamlined documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days Action required
elements.label: text

elements.text: 30 days Within five days of an evaluation decision, but in no event
elements.label: text

elements.text: later than 30 days following the receipt of a complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Response Package or streamlined
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation, the Servicer must send an Evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notice to the Borrower. The Evaluation Notice must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include approved model language for a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, or similar language drafted by the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 14 days The Servicer must allow the Borrower 14 days from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: date the Evaluation Notice is sent to accept the offer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursue a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 60 days (no later than 90
elements.label: text

elements.text: days)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must receive all necessary approvals (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: title, secondary lien(s), MI) and execute the deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure within 60 days of the date the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: communication to accept the offer was received.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is unable to execute the deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure within 60 days due to delays encountered as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: result of issues with title, secondary lien(s) or MI approval,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must continue working with the Borrower to
elements.label: text

elements.text: resolve these issues. In these instances, the Servicer will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: allotted an additional 30 days to execute the deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, and, during this time, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide weekly status updates to the Borrower (90 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum from the date the Borrower’s communication to
elements.label: text

elements.text: accept the offer was received to the date the deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure is executed).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain documentation of all communications to and from the Borrower,
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether verbal or written, and including status updates, either in the Mortgage file or in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s Servicing system. In addition, the Servicer must provide the information to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac for review upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 9101.3 for foreclosure suspension requirements for deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: when the First Complete Borrower Response Package is more than 37 days prior to a scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale and results in an offer to proceed with a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure. Refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 9102.5(c) and 9301.7(a) for foreclosure suspension requirements when the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been referred to foreclosure for either a Mortgage that is not secured by a Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: or a Mortgage that is secured by a Primary Residence and the Servicer is not evaluating the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure based on the First Complete Borrower Response
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package received more than 37 days prior to a scheduled foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9209.5: Deed-in-lieu of foreclosure transaction and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: processing requirements (11/01/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prior to approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Post-approval activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Resolve®
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Waiver of buydown funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property valuation requirements for deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order for the Freddie Mac Standard Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure (“deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure”) to be completed by the Servicer, the Servicer must work with and assist the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower to ensure that the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure transaction meets the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Prior to approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure the Borrower meets all eligibility requirements and negotiates a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: contribution, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain mortgage releases from all applicable subordinate mortgage holders as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer may authorize payment to subordinate mortgage holders in an aggregate
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount of six thousand dollars ($6,000.00). The subordinate mortgage holders must
elements.label: text

elements.text: not receive any other payments from the Borrower, in cash, promissory note or
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise, in connection with approval of the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If there are multiple subordinate mortgage holders, the Servicer has the discretion to
elements.label: text

elements.text: divide the payment among those mortgage holders so as to maximize the chances that
elements.label: text

elements.text: all subordinate mortgage holders will approve the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment of any amount to subordinate mortgage holders is contingent upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement by all mortgage holders to release their Mortgage and, if they are accepting
elements.label: text

elements.text: a payment from Freddie Mac, extinguish the indebtedness secured by the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, subordinate mortgage holders accepting payment from Freddie Mac must
elements.label: text

elements.text: agree in writing to waive all rights to seek a deficiency judgment against the Borrower. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: a subordinate mortgage holder releases its Mortgage to allow the deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure to close but does not extinguish the indebtedness, the mortgage holder will
elements.label: text

elements.text: not receive a payment from Freddie Mac. Regardless of whether payment is made to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: subordinate mortgage holder, the Servicer must obtain written commitment from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subordinate mortgage holder(s) to release the Mortgage(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: All payments made to subordinate mortgage holders must be documented, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation must be provided to Freddie Mac upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must have established written policies governing how subordinate mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments are paid and evidence of their agreement is provided to the Borrower. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure completed in accordance with this chapter, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: advance the amount to the subordinate mortgage holder or holders and request
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement for such advancement from Freddie Mac via PAID (Payments Automated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Only Mortgages or deeds of trust recorded in the land records and constituting a
elements.label: text

elements.text: valid lien against the property are eligible for a payment from Freddie Mac. Any and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: other types of liens, including, but not limited to, judgments, mechanic’s and
elements.label: text

elements.text: materialman’s liens and common interest association liens, are not eligible for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subordinate mortgage payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Approve and process a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure in compliance with all requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable mortgage insurance policies and any delegated authority granted to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer by the MI so as to preserve and not to impair existing mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverage, if any.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the MI indicates that it will curtail or deny a claim for any reason, including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, failure of the Servicer to comply with mortgage insurance conditions such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment of a Borrower contribution, the Servicer may not approve the deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure; however, the Servicer must submit the proposed deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Post-approval activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must obtain clear and marketable title to the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Contribute to any loss, according to the requirements in Section 9209.3(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Vacate the property and leave it in undamaged, broom-swept condition and provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer with the keys to the property at the time of conveyance, unless the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property is a 2- to 4-unit property and Freddie Mac allows one tenant to remain in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For leasehold Mortgages, obtain the consent of the fee simple landowner/lessor, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: required under the lease, and provide evidence of consent to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Execute a personal property release for any personal property the Borrower has left at
elements.label: text

elements.text: or in the Mortgaged Premises. The Servicer may use Exhibit 100, Personal Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Release, as a template and revise it as necessary to comply with applicable law or to
elements.label: text

elements.text: incorporate it into the Servicer’s own forms.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must obtain the executed deed-in-lieu of foreclosure and all other required
elements.label: text

elements.text: deed-in-lieu of foreclosure documents (including the personal property release). For
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasehold Mortgages, the Servicer must obtain either an assignment of the lease or a new
elements.label: text

elements.text: lease of the same priority.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Resolve
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must use Resolve to submit deed-in-lieu of foreclosure transactions on Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac-owned Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Servicer must use Resolve in accordance with (A) the requirements in this section, (B)
elements.label: text

elements.text: the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure and other instructions provided in Resolve Online Help and
elements.label: text

elements.text: any other documentation and (C) any other applicable provisions of the Guide, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 2401.1 and 2404.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer is unable to complete a submission via Resolve, the Servicer should call
elements.label: text

elements.text: Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Waiver of buydown funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must waive reimbursement of any buydown funds, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Property valuation requirements for deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on the information provided by the Servicer, Resolve will determine if there is an
elements.label: text

elements.text: existing valid valuation in Freddie Mac systems. If one is not available, Resolve will use the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information provided by the Servicer to order a BPO.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9209.6: Closing, reporting and remittance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements for deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting requirements and settlement data submissions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remittance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Reporting requirements and settlement data submissions
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Report the deed execution in Resolve® within one Business Day of receiving the
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed deed or lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Forward to Freddie Mac within five Business Days of receiving the executed deed or
elements.label: text

elements.text: lease:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s contribution, if applicable (see Directory 5)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a leasehold Mortgage, a copy of the lease via e-mail (see Directory 6)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the signed personal property release via e-mail (see Directory 6)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Report the Mortgage to Freddie Mac via the Freddie Mac Loan Level Reporting tool (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) as a Transfer to REO by the end of the Accounting Cycle in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the Servicer receives the executed deed or lease and ensure that the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ending gross UPB is the ending balance of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Principal due field is zero
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ REO acquisition date is the date the deed or lease was executed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ DDLPI reflects the due date of the last fully paid installment
elements.label: text

elements.text: If any of these data elements are incorrect, the Servicer should contact its investor
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting specialist or Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Once the Servicer reports the Mortgage as a Transfer to REO through the Loan Level
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting tool, Resolve will automatically settle the deed-in-lieu transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Remittance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Remit any funds, including funds contributed by the Borrower, via wire transfer or check to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) following completion of the reporting requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9209.6(a)(3) but no later than six Business Days of receipt of the executed deed or lease.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Other requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Ensure that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Clear and marketable title, or for a leasehold Mortgage, a marketable real estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasehold interest is conveyed to Freddie Mac; issuance or purchase of a letter of
elements.label: text

elements.text: indemnity, title insurance or similar form of indemnification does not constitute, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: may not be used in lieu of, provision of clear and marketable title to the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The deed or lease is submitted for recordation within five Business Days of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s receipt of the executed deed or lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The recorded deed or lease is e-mailed to Freddie Mac (see Directory 6) within one
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day after the Servicer receives it from the recorder’s office
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The recorded deed or lease and all other required deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents (including the personal property release) are maintained in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file and available to Freddie Mac upon request
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Request reimbursement for any applicable expenses, in accordance with Chapter 9701.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For expenses incurred on Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA or
elements.label: text

elements.text: RHS not subject to recourse or indemnification, expense reimbursement will occur after
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac receives the claim payment from the applicable entity and determines that
elements.label: text

elements.text: all other requirements for reimbursement have been satisfied. (Refer to Section 9603.2(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: on FHA/VA/RHS claim filing and Section 9603.2(d) on receipt of claim payment.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Release the First Lien within the time frame required by the applicable State or local law
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the delivery of the deed or lease of the Mortgaged Premises. If local or State law
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not require release within a specified time frame, the Servicer must submit the lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: release for recordation within 30 Business Days of the receipt of the deed or lease and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Report the acquisition of the property to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements in Section 8106.2(b) on IRS Form 1099-A, Acquisition
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Abandonment of Secured Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Release of deficiency if participants have acted in good faith and in compliance with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has acted in good faith and in compliance with the Guide and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable local, State and federal laws, then Freddie Mac will not pursue the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the entire amount owed under the current Mortgage. Freddie Mac will accept the deed
elements.label: text

elements.text: or lease and the cash contribution by the Borrower, and the Servicer must release the lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the Mortgaged Premises and mark the previous Note as canceled. For a deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure completed with respect to an eMortgage (as defined in Section 1402.1(b)), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must also comply with the requirements in Section 1402.5(c)(iii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, if the Borrower had knowledge of and/or consented to a transaction that was
elements.label: text

elements.text: not in compliance with the Guide and all applicable local, State and federal laws, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac reserves its rights to pursue any and all participants, including, but not limited to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, for the full amount owed under the Mortgage, the amount of any other loss or
elements.label: text

elements.text: damage Freddie Mac may have suffered and other costs and expenses, including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, attorneys’ fees.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. If the Mortgage is covered by mortgage insurance and the MI has not provided a
elements.label: text

elements.text: delegation of authority to the Servicer or Freddie Mac, fax a copy of the approval from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the MI to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) within two Business Days of the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: receipt of the MI’s approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. For Cooperative Share Loans, comply with the requirements in Section 8801.3(d)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9210: Charge-off
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 9210.1: Charge-off overview and recommendation
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: process (10/08/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ What is a charge-off?
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When a Servicer must recommend a charge-off
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submitting a recommendation for a charge-off to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s decision about a Servicer’s charge-off recommendation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) What is a charge-off?
elements.label: text

elements.text: A charge-off ceases collection efforts on a Mortgage when all appropriate measures have
elements.label: text

elements.text: been exhausted to collect on the delinquent Mortgage and the Servicer has deemed the debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: to be uncollectible or that a foreclosure should not be completed. In most cases, a charge-off
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be accompanied by a lien release and cancelation of the Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A charge-off may be appropriate in varying situations, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. A charge-off of a balance remaining after a short payoff. A short payoff may result from,
elements.label: text

elements.text: among other things:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A negotiated settlement with the Borrower, a legal settlement or an agreement to
elements.label: text

elements.text: resolve a legal dispute
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A settlement related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Condominium Project or Cooperative Project (see Chapter 8801 for special
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirements for Cooperative Share Loans) termination; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A unit of a Condominium Project or Cooperative Project that is damaged and its
elements.label: text

elements.text: repair is not feasible
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A court order
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A bankruptcy cramdown (see Section 9401.2(e) regarding completing and
elements.label: text

elements.text: transmitting the final terms of a confirmed bankruptcy cramdown to Freddie Mac via
elements.label: text

elements.text: the “Custom Modification” functionality in Resolve®); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A receivership
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. A full charge-off of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage that is secured by a Mortgaged Premises that is subject to property
elements.label: text

elements.text: seizure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage that is not subject to a recourse or repurchase obligation under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents, and it is not practical or possible to complete foreclosure or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other enforcement of Freddie Mac’s rights under the Note or Mortgage and/or to
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain clear title to the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A low balance Mortgage that is delinquent and Freddie Mac has determined to not
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete a foreclosure of the Mortgage. (See Section 9210.1(b) for more details.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage or Cooperative Share Loan that is secured by a unit of a Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project or Cooperative Project, respectively, and:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The project is not presently economically viable;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The project has been terminated; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The unit is damaged and its repair is not feasible
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage that is secured by a Mortgaged Premises for which has been impacted by
elements.label: text

elements.text: a natural or manmade disaster, or other like circumstance, and rebuilding on the land
elements.label: text

elements.text: is determined by the Servicer to be impracticable or impossible
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage that is secured by undeveloped land or any above-grade primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: structure has been demolished and removed by the Borrower, jurisdiction or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: party; and the vacant land (and any remaining outbuilding, if applicable) has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: protected from waste, damage and vandalism
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage that is secured by a Mortgaged Premises that a third party is willing to
elements.label: text

elements.text: accept any risk of liability if it becomes the owner of the property. (Note: This may
elements.label: text

elements.text: also include a charge-off of a balance left over after a short payoff as the result of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: negotiated settlement with the third party.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage that is secured by a Mortgaged Premises that has been identified as
elements.label: text

elements.text: posing a Risk of Property Ownership. (See Section 9202.2(b) for more details)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) When a Servicer must recommend a charge-off
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must recommend a charge-off to Freddie Mac instead of proceeding with
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure when the debt that is secured by the Mortgaged Premises is deemed uncollectible
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or any of the following situations in numbered paragraphs 1 through 9 below exist:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. A short payoff has been approved by Freddie Mac and a balance is left over (see Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Mortgaged Premises is subject to property seizure (see Section 9210.1(a))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. For any Mortgage not subject to recourse or repurchase obligations under the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents, the Servicer determines that it is not practical or possible to complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure or other enforcement of Freddie Mac’s rights and/or to obtain clear title to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises (see Section 9210.1(a))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Servicer identifies a low balance Mortgage with a UPB that is $5,000 or less,
elements.label: text

elements.text: regardless of the equity in the property, and the criteria of both (a) and (b) below are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Prior to the Mortgage becoming 120 days delinquent or the Mortgage maturing prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to becoming 120 days delinquent, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Solicit a payoff or other form of workout from the Borrower and respond to all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower inquiries and requests about the Mortgage; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that payments have been correctly applied to the Mortgage. (In certain
elements.label: text
