elements.text: circumstances, Freddie Mac may request that the Servicer provide documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: to substantiate that payments have been applied correctly to the Mortgage. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac determines that the payments were not applied correctly, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may be required to satisfy the affected debt.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) The following eligibility requirements must be met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The property is owner-occupied
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property inspections continue to show the property maintenance to be both up to
elements.label: text

elements.text: neighborhood standards and compliant with municipal requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is at least 120 days delinquent or has matured prior to becoming
elements.label: text

elements.text: 120 days delinquent; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower is not currently performing under a relief or workout arrangement
elements.label: text

elements.text: or a bankruptcy plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: In the event the Servicer identifies a Mortgage with a UPB that is greater than
elements.label: text

elements.text: $5,000, but all other criteria in both (a) and (b) are met, the Servicer may make a
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommendation for a charge-off to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The Mortgage or Cooperative Share Loan (see Chapter 8801 for special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Cooperative Share Loans) is secured by a unit of a Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project or Cooperative Project, respectively, and:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The project is not presently economically viable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The project has been terminated; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The unit is damaged and its repair is not feasible (see Section 9210.1(a))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. The Mortgaged Premises has been impacted by a natural or man-made disaster, or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: like circumstance, and rebuilding on the land is determined by the Servicer to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: impracticable or impossible (see Section 9210.1(a))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. The Mortgage is secured by undeveloped land or any above-grade primary structure has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been demolished and removed by the Borrower, jurisdiction or other party and the vacant
elements.label: text

elements.text: land (and any remaining outbuilding, if applicable) has been protected from waste,
elements.label: text

elements.text: damage and vandalism (see Section 9210.1(a))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. A third party is willing to accept any risk of liability if it becomes the owner of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property (see Section 9210.1(a))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. The Mortgaged Premises poses a Risk of Property Ownership to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9202.2(b))
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the above requirements, if the Mortgage is a matured Mortgage, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must comply with the requirements of Section 8303.1(c) with respect to the payoff of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: matured Mortgage and only submit a charge-off request if the Mortgage is delinquent at time
elements.label: text

elements.text: of maturity, or becomes delinquent following maturity and meets any of the above criteria.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Submitting a recommendation for a charge-off to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must use the Resolve user interface (UI) to submit a charge-off recommendation to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac. Servicers that elect to submit charge-off recommendations via the Resolve UI
elements.label: text

elements.text: must use Resolve in accordance with (A) the requirements in this section, (B) the charge-off
elements.label: text

elements.text: and other instructions provided in Resolve Online Help and any other documentation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C) any other applicable provisions of the Guide, including Sections 2401.1 and 2404.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must take the steps listed below in this section when it recommends a charge￾off of a Mortgage to Freddie Mac. Before making the recommendation, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: have filed and settled any property insurance claim(s) with the applicable property insurer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To submit a charge-off recommendation, the Servicer must complete the charge-off template
elements.label: text

elements.text: and upload the following via Resolve:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. A detailed explanation of the Servicer’s recommendation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Documentation describing the condition of the property, including copies of the most
elements.label: text

elements.text: recent six consecutive months of property inspection reports with photographs. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: charge-off recommendations on low balance Mortgages (see Section 9210.1(b)), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may submit less than six consecutive months of property inspection reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: depending on the level of Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Servicer has previously provided this information in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 8403.1(a) and (b), the Servicer does not need to submit this information again as
elements.label: text

elements.text: part of the charge-off recommendation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. A Hazard Distressed BPO obtained through BPOdirect®, if applicable, if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommendation to charge off a Mortgage is due to condemnation, disaster or other Risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Property Ownership (see Section 9202.2(b)). The Hazard Distressed BPO must be less
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 90 days old on the date the Servicer makes the recommendation to charge off the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, unless expressly approved by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. A payoff statement, when applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. A copy of any property insurance claims filed, including a copy of either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An explanation of benefits and proof of payment received by the Servicer on any paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: claim(s); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any notification documenting reason(s) for non-payment of a claim and if an appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: was filed by the Servicer that was also denied for payment of a claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. A copy of any estimates that may have been obtained by the Servicer for necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: repairs
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. If the Mortgage is secured by a Manufactured Home, the documentation specified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9301.2(f)(iii) or 9301.2(f)(iv) to evidence that the Manufactured Home and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: land are real property under the laws of the State where the property is located. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home is located in a certificate of title State (see Section 5703.4(f)(iii)),
elements.label: text

elements.text: then the Servicer must provide the documentation specified in Section 9301.2(f)(v).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will contact the Servicer should it require additional documentation following
elements.label: text

elements.text: the submission of a recommendation to charge off a Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Freddie Mac’s decision about a Servicer’s charge-off recommendation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will review the documentation the Servicer submits and make a decision to
elements.label: text

elements.text: approve or deny the charge-off request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Approval of request
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will notify the Servicer if the charge-off recommendation is approved and
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the lien has been released through Resolve. Upon approval of the request, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must not incur any new expenses for taxes, insurance, property preservation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: legal fees or any other fees or costs if Escrow is insufficient to pay charges when due
elements.label: text

elements.text: without Freddie Mac’s prior approval (see Directory 5).
elements.label: text

elements.text: In most cases, when Freddie Mac approves a charge-off request, Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: instruct the Servicer to release its lien on the property and cancel the Note as part of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: closing of an approved charge-off request (see Section 9210.2). However, in situations in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which future funds are anticipated (e.g., when Freddie Mac or the Servicer is negotiating
elements.label: text

elements.text: a settlement with the Borrower or a third party), Freddie Mac will retain its lien on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property and will instruct the Servicer not to release the lien or cancel the Note on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Request Details Screen in Resolve until such time that funds are received by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Denial of request
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac denies the charge-off request, Freddie Mac will provide the Servicer the
elements.label: text

elements.text: response in Resolve, stating the reason Freddie Mac denied the request together with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: course of action the Servicer must take to resolve the Delinquency, including the
elements.label: text

elements.text: resumption of normal default Servicing activity, including all Escrow advances, property
elements.label: text

elements.text: preservation and legal activities, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9210.2: Closing, reporting and remittance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements for charge-offs (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lien release requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Settlement data submissions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Charging-off the Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remittance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Closing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requesting reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Lien release requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac instructs the Servicer not to complete a lien release as part of the approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: charge-off request, upon receiving a request from a Freddie Mac-approved vendor (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9601.1(a) for details on this process), the Servicer must prepare and submit all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following documentation to the Freddie Mac-approved vendor:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Original Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Original Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the assignment of the Security Instrument to “Federal Home Loan Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation” sent to the local recorder’s office; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the original loan application
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must forward the recorded assignment to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) when
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer has received it from the recorder’s office. When sending the documentation to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac-approved vendor, the Servicer must only send the items listed above, not entire
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event Freddie Mac approves a charge-off request and instructs the Servicer to release
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lien on the property and cancel the Note, the Servicer must prepare and execute a
elements.label: text

elements.text: satisfaction of Note and/or release of lien. For Cooperative Share Loans, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: also comply with the requirements in Section 8801.3(e).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within two Business Days of the workout approval reflected in Resolve®, following receipt
elements.label: text

elements.text: of funds when a short payoff has been approved by Freddie Mac, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Reinstate the Mortgage if it was inactivated; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Report the Mortgage as a “Short Sale/Charge-off/Make-whole” in the Loan Level
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting tool (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) and ensure that the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ending gross UPB is zero
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Principal due field is completed with the gross unpaid ending balance of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff date is the workout approval date reflected in Resolve; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ DDLPI reflects the due date of the last fully paid installment
elements.label: text

elements.text: If any of these data elements are incorrect, the Servicer should contact its investor reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: specialist or call Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Settlement data submissions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Following completion of the reporting and remittance requirements, Resolve will
elements.label: text

elements.text: automatically settle the charge-off transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Charging-off the Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: The amount that Freddie Mac has determined to be charged off will be reflected on the Draft
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report. The Servicer must review the Draft Report and report any discrepancies between its
elements.label: text

elements.text: records and the amount on the Draft Report to Freddie Mac via the Freddie Mac Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Data Corrections tool (see Exhibit 88) within the same Accounting Cycle in which Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac posts the amount to the Draft Report. Servicers may access the Draft Report through the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash Manager tool (see Exhibit 88).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When reporting a discrepancy, Servicers must input the calculation used to determine the
elements.label: text

elements.text: variance and upload any documentation to support the request in the Servicing Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corrections tool.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Discrepancies submitted after the Accounting Cycle in which the initial adjustment is posted
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Draft Report closes will be processed at Freddie Mac’s discretion and may be subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a contract noncompliance and contract change compensatory fee (see Section 8303.5(i)). If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the postsettlement correction request is denied, the Servicer may be liable for any additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: losses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Remittance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Following completion of the reporting requirements in Section 9210.2(c) but no later than
elements.label: text

elements.text: three Business Days after workout approval reflected in Resolve or following receipt of
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds when a short-payoff has been approved by Freddie Mac, the Servicer must submit any
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds received on a Mortgage that has an approved charge-off request via wire transfer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: check to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5), regardless of whether the lien is released or not.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Closing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within seven Business Days of the workout approval date reflected in Resolve, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Notify the Borrower in writing that the Servicer will no longer service the Mortgage. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: written notice must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac's nine-digit loan number and the Servicer's loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information that either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The lien on the property is being released and the Note is being canceled; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The lien is not being released, the Note is not being canceled, the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: being assigned to Freddie Mac and the Borrower remains financially obligated to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Note and Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Instructions that the Borrower remains responsible for paying property taxes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: property insurance premiums and that such payments should be made directly to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: taxing authority or insurer, even if the Mortgage had an Escrow account prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: charge-off
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. If the Mortgage had an Escrow account prior to the charge-off, the Servicer must notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: the local taxing authority in writing to send all tax bills directly to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Requesting reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order for the Servicer to be reimbursed for charge-off related expenses, the charge-off
elements.label: text

elements.text: must first be accepted and settled in Resolve. The Servicer must request reimbursement for
elements.label: text

elements.text: any applicable expenses, in accordance with the requirements in Chapter 9701.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9211: Third Party Mortgage Assistance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9211.1: Mortgage assistance funds (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section highlights the requirements relating to mortgage assistance administered by State
elements.label: text

elements.text: and local governments along with other third-party organizations. The following information is
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General requirements for mortgage assistance funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower authorization requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages with credit enhancements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Another relief or workout arrangement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Foreclosure actions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General requirements for mortgage assistance funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The facilitating organization determines Borrower eligibility criteria and underwrites the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower. Unless permitted by the organization, Servicers may not determine Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility or communicate a determination of eligibility or qualification for mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance to a Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must accept such mortgage assistance funds on behalf of a Borrower as though they
elements.label: text

elements.text: were from the Borrower and apply the funds in accordance with the Security Instrument, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide and applicable law. Should the funds received be insufficient to bring the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: current, refer to the sections below on partial reinstatements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional reinstatement requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Types of reinstatement Section 9203.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: When to accept full reinstatement Section 9203.1(d)
elements.label: text

elements.text: When to accept partial reinstatement of a Mortgage in foreclosure Section 9203.1(e)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting and processing the reinstatement Section 9203.1(f)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reimbursement of expenses related to reinstatements Section 9203.1(g)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If payment represents only a partial payment, the Servicer must not waive rights under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage to collect the unpaid amount and must continue collection efforts and attempt to
elements.label: text

elements.text: establish quality right party contact as outlined in Chapters 9101 and 9102.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For Borrowers where mortgage assistance funds result in a partial prepayment, refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 8103.3(d) and 8303.2(a)(iii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Borrower authorization requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with a Borrower’s request for mortgage assistance, Servicers must receive
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorization from each Borrower to release his or her nonpublic personal financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: information to an authorized mortgage assistance organization. The Borrower’s authorization
elements.label: text

elements.text: to release his or her nonpublic personal financial information to the mortgage assistance
elements.label: text

elements.text: organization and all communications that include a Borrower’s nonpublic personal
elements.label: text

elements.text: information must comply with Section 1301.2 and all applicable laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Mortgages with credit enhancements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must comply with the requirements of, and obtain approvals as necessary from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA, VA, RHS and/or MI. Credit enhancement on a Mortgage must stay in place regardless
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Borrower’s participation in a mortgage assistance program.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Another relief or workout arrangement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must not deny or delay consideration of a Borrower for a relief or workout option
elements.label: text

elements.text: pending approval for mortgage assistance. If a Borrower is currently performing under a
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief or workout arrangement that has not been completed and the Borrower requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance from a mortgage assistance program, the Servicer must permit the Borrower to
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue with the relief or workout arrangement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As noted in Section 9212.1(a), any funds received from the mortgage assistance program
elements.label: text

elements.text: should be treated as funds from the Borrower and applied in accordance with the Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument, the Guide and applicable law. Servicers should refer to Chapters 9203 through
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9210 for relief and workout options guidance accordingly.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For streamlined evaluations for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification®, the Servicer must refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: guidance provided in Section 9206.1(c)(iii) in the event the Mortgage is reinstated, but in no
elements.label: text

elements.text: instance must any Freddie Mac Flex Modification Trial Period Plan already evaluated for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: offered be revoked as a result of reinstatement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must cancel an active forbearance plan, an active repayment plan or a payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: deferral that has not yet been completed if the Mortgage is fully reinstated as a result of
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage assistance funds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower requests additional assistance following reinstatement, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluate the Borrower for relief and workout options in accordance with the evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: hierarchy in Section 9201.2 and with the requirements for the specific relief and workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: options. Refer to the following Guide sections for details on additional workout options:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional workout options
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reinstatements and relief options Chapter 9203
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac workout options Chapter 9204
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Affordable Modification ProgramSM Chapter 9205
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modifications Chapter 9206
elements.label: text

elements.text: Workout Mortgage assumption Chapter 9207
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale Chapter 9208
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Standard Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure Chapter 9209
elements.label: text

elements.text: Charge-off Chapter 9210
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Foreclosure actions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may postpone foreclosure activity in accordance with the Guide and applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: for up to 45 days if they have been notified that the Borrower is participating in a mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance program and they have a good faith belief that the participation with the program
elements.label: text

elements.text: will cure the Borrower’s Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may extend the suspension of foreclosure actions beyond 45 days, as necessary,
elements.label: text

elements.text: to facilitate the processing of the mortgage assistance, provided that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ It continues to validate with the mortgage assistance provider that the Borrower’s status
elements.label: text

elements.text: has not changed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The action is in compliance with its agreement with the mortgage assistance provider;
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ It obtains Freddie Mac’s prior approval for any suspension beyond 60 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must submit approval requests for foreclosure activity suspension beyond 60 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Foreclosures@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should refer to appropriate Guide sections on preforeclosure activities, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 9301.2(a) and 9301.7(b). Servicers must note the reason for the postponement in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file (e.g., third-party mortgage assistance). In the event a foreclosure sale occurs,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer’s performance will be measured against Freddie Mac’s State foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: timelines (refer to Exhibit 83, Freddie Mac State Foreclosure Timelines).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are reminded that if they postpone foreclosure activity, they can resume foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: activity without delay if the mortgage assistance program and/or any loss mitigation solution
elements.label: text

elements.text: is unable to resolve the Borrower’s Delinquency. Servicers are further reminded that when
elements.label: text

elements.text: communicating a postponement request for a pending foreclosure sale to foreclosure counsel,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must identify the reason for the postponement (i.e., third-party mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a notice of trustee/sheriff sale has been recorded and the trustee/sheriff sale is scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: less than seven days from the date the Servicer is notified of Borrower approval for mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance, the Servicer is not required to accept the mortgage assistance payment. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer should refer to Section 9301.7(b)(ii), the terms of the Note and applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordingly for further guidance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Reporting to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must be able to readily identify any Borrower who receives third-party
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage assistance funds from any State or local governmental agency, such as a housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: finance agency or its designee. The Servicer must be able to provide this information to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9301: Foreclosure
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 9301.1: Foreclosures overview and Servicer
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: responsibilities (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Foreclosures on Freddie Mac Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General Servicer requirements for Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Foreclosures on Freddie Mac Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must refer to, manage and complete foreclosure in accordance with this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: when there is no available alternative to foreclosure. Additionally, Freddie Mac requires the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer to manage the foreclosure process to acquire clear and marketable title to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property in a cost-effective, expeditious and efficient manner.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) General Servicer requirements for Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: When following the requirements provided in this chapter while processing Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default Legal Matters, the Servicer must comply with:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The terms and conditions of the Mortgage documents, including the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Applicable federal, State and local laws and customs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements of the FHA, VA, RHS or MI, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Guide and other Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Among other rights available under applicable law and the Guide, Freddie Mac reserves the
elements.label: text

elements.text: right to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Select the foreclosure counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Direct and manage the actions taken by the foreclosure counsel, on a case-by-case or
elements.label: text

elements.text: jurisdiction-wide basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Assess compensatory fees and/or seek repayment of losses sustained due to errors,
elements.label: text

elements.text: omissions or delays by the Servicer or its agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Limit the amount of reimbursement for attorney fees if those fees exceed the expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: limits in Exhibit 57A, Approved Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation, Postponement Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Title Expenses, or the fees commonly charged for similar services in the area where
elements.label: text

elements.text: the services are being performed
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac determines that the Servicer has directly or indirectly passed or charged to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac any non-reimbursable expenses itemized in Section 9701.4(a) or charged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac for Servicing Contract obligations covered by the Servicing Spread (as set forth
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 8105.1(c)) or in violation of Section 9501.4(c)(i), then Freddie Mac may pursue
elements.label: text

elements.text: any or all the remedies specified in the Guide and the other Purchase Documents, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.2: Preforeclosure and referral requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Preforeclosure referral account review
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Referral to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to refer a Mortgage to foreclosure – Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to refer a Mortgage to foreclosure for a non-Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Approving referral to foreclosure on a First Lien Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Referral to foreclosure documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Preforeclosure referral account review
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Review of the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within 15 days prior to referral to foreclosure, the Servicer must review the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: to verify that it made every attempt to achieve quality right party contact in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Section 9102.3, and there is no approved payment arrangement or pending
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure offer for which the Borrower response period has not expired.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event that the Servicer finds that there is an approved payment arrangement or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: pending offer for an alternative to foreclosure for which the Borrower response period
elements.label: text

elements.text: has not expired, then the Servicer must not refer the Mortgage to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must document the results of its review in the Mortgage file or servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Prior approval required from Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must seek Freddie Mac’s approval to refer the Mortgage to foreclosure if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property inspections or any other information provided to the Servicer indicates that
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeding with referral may not be in Freddie Mac’s best interest or that the property is
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered a distressed property (see Section 9301.2(e) for a list of affected property
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions). See also Section 8801.4(a) regarding prior approval required from Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac for Cooperative Share Loans. Servicers must send approval requests to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosures@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Chapter 8403 for additional Servicing Contract requirements for Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages on distressed properties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Chapter 9101 for additional Servicing Contract requirements for Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages that secure Primary Residences.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Referral to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Referral to foreclosure is the submission of a Mortgage case and appropriate foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation to foreclosure counsel after taking all appropriate actions to accelerate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage. Refer to Sections 9301.2(c) and 9301.2(d) for requirements for when to refer a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) When to refer a Mortgage to foreclosure – Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must refer a Mortgage to foreclosure after expiration of the breach letter,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but no earlier than 151 days from the DDLPI (121st day of Delinquency). However,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may not refer a Mortgage to foreclosure if prohibited by applicable law or one
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the following situations applies:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There is an approved payment arrangement or an alternative to foreclosure; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A complete Borrower Response Package is received and the Servicer is still within
elements.label: text

elements.text: the 30-day evaluation time period for evaluating the package as prescribed in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has extended an offer for an alternative to foreclosure and the period for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s response has not expired; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower has accepted an offer for an alternative to foreclosure and is
elements.label: text

elements.text: performing in accordance with its terms; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The time period for the Borrower to exercise a right to appeal a denial of a Trial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Period Plan or loan modification (per Section 9101.2) has not expired, the Servicer is
elements.label: text

elements.text: reviewing the Borrower’s appeal, or the Borrower’s time period to respond to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s decision on the appeal and any acceptance period for an alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure offered as part of that decision has not expired
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 9101.3 for additional foreclosure suspension requirements when the First
elements.label: text

elements.text: Complete Borrower Response Package is received more than 37 days prior to a scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To accept an offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower’s notification to the Servicer that he or she intends to accept an offer of an
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure may be demonstrated as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verbal notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Written notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a payment is required to legally accept an alternative to foreclosure offer, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower does not communicate a verbal or written rejection of the alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure offer, the Servicer must postpone referral to foreclosure, where legally
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted, at least through the last day of the month in which the first payment is due
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the terms of the alternative to foreclosure offer or until the deadline for Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptance has passed. If the Servicer receives the first payment timely in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the terms of a Trial Period Plan, repayment plan or forbearance plan, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must delay the next legal action unless the Borrower breaches the plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA or RHS or covered by
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must comply with the foreclosure referral requirements of the FHA, VA,
elements.label: text

elements.text: RHS or MI, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Performance measurement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of the reason for the delay in referral to foreclosure beyond 151 days from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: DDLPI (121st day of Delinquency), the Servicer’s performance will be measured against
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s State foreclosure timelines (refer to Exhibit 83, Freddie Mac State
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure Timelines).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) When to refer a Mortgage to foreclosure for a non-Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must refer a Mortgage to foreclosure no later than 150 days from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: DDLPI (120th day of Delinquency) unless the pre-referral accounts review indicates that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There is an approved payment arrangement or an alternative to foreclosure; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A complete Borrower Response Package is received and the Servicer is still within
elements.label: text

elements.text: the 30-day evaluation time period for evaluating the package as prescribed in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has extended an offer for an alternative to foreclosure (except for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: offer for a Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale (“short sale”) or Freddie Mac Standard
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure (“deed-in-lieu of foreclosure”)), and the period for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s response has not expired; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower has accepted an offer for an alternative to foreclosure and is
elements.label: text

elements.text: performing in accordance with its terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Mortgage must be referred to foreclosure and foreclosure proceedings must
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue if the Servicer sends the Borrower an offer to pursue a short sale or deed-in-lieu
elements.label: text

elements.text: of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To accept an offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower’s notification to the Servicer that he or she intends to accept an offer to an
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure may be demonstrated as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verbal notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Written notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a payment is required to legally accept an alternative to foreclosure offer, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower does not communicate a verbal or written rejection of the alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure offer, the Servicer must postpone referral to foreclosure, where legally
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted, at least through the last day of the month in which the first payment is due
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the terms of the alternative to foreclosure offer or until the deadline for Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptance has passed. If the Servicer receives the first payment timely in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the terms of a Trial Period Plan, the Servicer must delay the next legal action unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower breaches the plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, unless Freddie Mac requires the Servicer to obtain Freddie Mac’s approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to referral to foreclosure (refer to Section 9301.2(f) for instances where Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac must approve the referral of the Mortgage to foreclosure), if the Borrower has
elements.label: text

elements.text: abandoned the property, then the Servicer may refer to foreclosure as soon as it is legally
elements.label: text

elements.text: possible to do so. The Servicer must send approval requests to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosures@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text
